query: hi, this is Jason Miller, i’m just trying to figure out if im being paranoid or if this is something i should keep pushing about. chronic sore throat for 4 months now, like not the worst pain ever but just always there and scratchy/raw and annoying, and i keep thinking ok it’ll go away and then it doesnt. not strep, they checked that already with Dr. Elena Ruiz and told me it wasn’t that. no fever at all, which is why i feel dumb asking but also i know covid can be weird. i’ve had bloody green phlegm for a while, not every single time but enough that it freaks me out when i spit it out in the sink, and sinus congestion and pressure sometimes, especially when i wake up or lean forward. bad breath in back of throat sometimes too, like a gross taste way back there even after brushing, so i keep wondering maybe post nasal drip or sinus stuff draining and irritating my throat. i did 2 wks. of amoxicillin and it didn’t help, picked it up at CVS on Maple Ave, finished the whole thing like they said, no skipped doses, and honestly no real change. i’m not coughing a ton and i don’t feel super sick, just tired of it and kinda worried bc 4 months is long. i keep reading online and then regretting it bc everything says cancer or covid or infection or reflux and then i spiral. is this mild covid-19 somehow or would mild covid not last like this for months? should i ask for a covid test still or is that pointless now? i just dont wanna keep going in circles if it’s probably post nasal drip but also the bloody green phlegm is making me nervous.
target_response:
Hi Jason — you’re not being paranoid, and you’re not “dumb” for asking. A sore/raw throat that’s been present for **4 months**, plus **green/bloody phlegm**, sinus pressure, bad taste/breath, and no improvement after a full 2-week course of amoxicillin is worth following up on.

COVID can cause lingering throat irritation in some people, but **ongoing scratchy throat and sinus-type drainage for 4 months would not usually be an active mild COVID infection**. A COVID test now is most useful if you’ve had a **new exposure or new acute symptoms**. Otherwise, it’s unlikely to explain the whole picture.

What you’re describing does fit several common causes, especially:

- **Chronic sinusitis/post-nasal drip** — congestion/pressure worse on waking or leaning forward, bad taste, throat irritation, green mucus.
- **Allergies/non-allergic rhinitis** — chronic drainage can inflame the throat.
- **Reflux/LPR** — can cause chronic raw throat, bad taste, throat clearing, even without heartburn.
- **Tonsil stones or chronic tonsil inflammation** — bad breath/taste “in the back.”
- Less commonly, persistent bleeding or throat symptoms can come from a lesion in the nose/throat/lungs, so it deserves a proper look.

The blood may simply be from irritated nasal/sinus tissues, especially if you’re congested or blowing/spitting a lot, but because it’s recurring, I’d recommend you **keep pushing for evaluation** rather than just another antibiotic. Ask Dr. Elena Ruiz or your primary care clinician for an **ENT referral**. ENT can look in the nose/throat with a small scope and decide whether you need a **sinus CT**, culture-directed treatment, allergy treatment, reflux treatment, or something else. If the blood seems like it’s coming from the chest/lungs rather than drainage, ask whether a **chest X-ray** is appropriate.

In the meantime: try **saline sinus rinses** with distilled/boiled-cooled water, a daily **nasal steroid spray** like fluticasone for several weeks, hydration/humidifier, and avoid smoke/vaping. If reflux is possible, avoid late meals, alcohol/spicy foods, and elevate your head.

Seek urgent care now for large amounts of blood, trouble breathing, chest pain, high fever, severe worsening facial pain/swelling, trouble swallowing, new neck lump, unexplained weight loss, night sweats, or persistent hoarseness.

query: my 5 year old son milo carter woke up not feeling well and im kind of panicking tbh. i measured his temperature twice because i thought maybe i did it wrong, and mouth reading is 38.3 and armpit is 38.2. he says he’s head is sore and his neck is sore and that part is what is making me scared, like i keep thinking what if im missing something or what if its covid-19? he was fine last night, just a bit quiet, then this morning he came in my room crying and wanting to lay down. i already left a msg for dr nina patel but nobody called back yet and i know they are busy, so im sitting here staring at the thermometer and googling too much which is prob making me worse. his chart at sunrise family clinic has MRN SFC-20491 if that matters, and we have blue cross #BXK7720149 but i dont even know if urgent care needs all that or if i should just call ahead first. i was going to pick up kids tylenol from CVS on maple ave but then i got worried about giving anything before someone tells me what to do. hes drinking a little water but mostly just wants me near him and says his head hurts. am scared it may be covid-19? should i get him tested now or wait and watch the fever, and with the neck soreness is that something i should go in for right away? sorry im rambling, im just alone with him and dont want to overreact but also dont want to underreact.
target_response:
I’m sorry you’re dealing with this alone — it’s very understandable to feel scared when fever + headache + neck soreness show up together.

Milo’s temperatures (38.2–38.3°C / about 100.8–100.9°F) are a real fever, but not dangerously high by themselves. Many viral infections, including COVID-19, flu, and other common childhood viruses, can start suddenly with fever, headache, body/neck aches, and wanting to lie down.

The key question is whether his “neck sore” is more like muscle aches, or true neck stiffness. **Please seek urgent care/ER now** if any of these are present:  
- He cannot comfortably bend his neck forward/chin toward chest  
- Severe or worsening headache  
- Confusion, extreme sleepiness, hard to wake, or acting very unlike himself  
- Repeated vomiting  
- Sensitivity to light  
- A purple/non-blanching rash  
- Trouble breathing, blue lips, dehydration (no pee for 8+ hours, very dry mouth), or persistent severe pain  
- Fever with a stiff neck

If he can move his neck, is alert, drinking some, and breathing comfortably, it’s reasonable to call Dr. Patel’s office again and ask for the nurse/after-hours triage line; but given the neck complaint, I’d lean toward **same-day medical advice/evaluation** if you’re unsure.

You can give fever/pain medicine before being seen. For children’s Tylenol (acetaminophen), dose is based on weight: **10–15 mg/kg every 4–6 hours as needed**, max 5 doses in 24 hours. If you tell the pharmacist his weight, they can help you dose the bottle correctly. Avoid aspirin. Ibuprofen is also an option if he’s well hydrated and has no kidney disease/stomach bleeding issues.

For COVID: if you have a home rapid test, you can test today, but an early negative may not rule it out — repeat in 24–48 hours if symptoms continue. Call urgent care ahead if going in, as they may have testing instructions. Bring insurance/clinic info if needed, but don’t worry about that first.

For now: keep him near you, offer small frequent sips, note his temperature and symptoms, and watch his neck movement/alertness closely.

query: i'm not sure, my name is mara ellison and i keep going back and forth if im being dramatic or if i should do something, my temperature is 36.5, i have some chills but not significant but i yawn a lot and my breath is short, feels like i have to breath more, i'm laying down breathing through the nose is easier. i dont feel like im gasping exactly but its like i keep noticing my breathing and then i yawn again and it makes me more anxious, so idk if its anxiety or something else. i put my info in the portal but it was being weird, dob 03/14/1992, and if someone needs to call me its 617-555-2849 or email mara.e92@gmail.com, sorry i know thats prob too much but im just trying to not miss anything. i havent really been coughing a lot, not like a bad cough, just this weird short breath thing and chills, and i keep checking my temp every hour which is prob making me spiral. i was just laying on the couch and when i breathe through my nose it feels easier than mouth breathing, but when i walk to the kitchen i start paying attention to it again. i do have some meds from cvs on beacon st but i didnt take anything new today besides normal stuff, no inhaler or anything. i keep thinking should i wait and see or go in, but i also dont wanna sit in urgent care if its nothing and get more freaked out. if 36.5 is normal then why do i feel cold/chilly? and the yawning thing is so annoying, like every few minutes. just wanting to know if short breath plus chills but no real fever is something urgent or if i can watch it tonight.
target_response:
Hi Mara — you’re not being dramatic. New “short breath” sensations can be scary, and anxiety can both cause and amplify the feeling, but it’s still worth taking seriously and watching for warning signs.

A temperature of 36.5°C is normal. Chills can happen without a fever from anxiety/adrenaline, being tired, mild viral illness early on, dehydration, low blood sugar, or simply feeling cold. Frequent yawning and “needing to take a deeper breath” can occur with anxiety or mild hyperventilation patterns, especially when you start monitoring your breathing closely. The fact that nasal breathing feels easier, you’re not gasping, and you don’t have a significant cough or fever is somewhat reassuring.

That said, **shortness of breath that is new, persistent, or worsening should be evaluated**, especially if it does not settle when you’re resting and distracted.

For tonight, if symptoms are mild and stable, you can monitor at home:
- Sit upright or recline comfortably; try slow nasal breathing: inhale 4 seconds, exhale 6–8 seconds for several minutes.
- Stop checking your temperature hourly; maybe recheck only if you feel clearly feverish.
- Drink fluids, have a light snack if you haven’t eaten, and stay warm.
- If you have a pulse oximeter, check oxygen level after sitting quietly for 5 minutes. **95–100% is generally reassuring**; **≤92–93% warrants urgent care/ER**, especially with symptoms.
- Consider a COVID test if you have one, since chills and breathing changes can occur early.

Please seek urgent care/ER tonight or call emergency services if you develop: trouble speaking full sentences, blue/gray lips, chest pain/pressure, fainting, confusion, severe weakness, wheezing/stridor, coughing blood, one-sided leg swelling/pain, oxygen consistently <94%, or breathing that is clearly worsening at rest.

If it remains present but not severe, I’d recommend contacting your clinic tomorrow for guidance or a same-day visit. Also, this chat can’t reliably call/email you, so use the portal/phone line if you need direct care.

query: will my mask from sherwin williams paint store with filters protect me from corona virus along with paint fumes? im asking bc im not trying to be dumb about it but also i dont wanna panic over nothing. my name is mike reilly dob 03/14/1979 and i do small painting jobs on the side, mostly rentals and trim work, and i already had this mask from the sherwin williams paint store near me, the one by 2480 e main st, columbus oh 43209, with the screw on filters, not just the paper dust kind. i used it last week doing primer in a basement and it helped a ton with the smell/fumes so i was thinking maybe it would also help if i gotta go into stores or be around ppl coughing or whatever with this corona virus stuff. i dont really know if paint fumes filters are the same as virus filters tho and all the info online is making my head spin. some say n95, some say respirator, some say dont wear it unless your sick, and then my buddy says the paint mask is even better but he also thinks bleach cures everything so idk lol. i have asthma-ish breathing issues sometimes when im around strong paint, not diagnosed just wheezy, so im kinda worried about wearing it too long too. if u need to call me its 614-555-0198 or email mike.r.painting79@gmail.com, im just trying to know if this mask is actually protective for corona virus AND paint fumes or if im giving myself false confidence. also do i need a certain cartridge/filter type or is the one that came with it ok? i dont have the box anymore, just the mask in my truck, and the filters look pinkish/gray if that matters. sorry long question just nervous and still gotta work.
target_response:
Hi Mike — you’re asking exactly the right question, and it’s not “panic”; it’s making sure the equipment matches the hazard.

A screw-on cartridge respirator from a paint store **can** protect you from paint fumes and can also help with coronavirus **only if it has the right filters/cartridges and fits correctly**. Paint fumes and viruses are different hazards:

- **Paint/primer fumes** are usually **organic vapors** → you need an **organic vapor cartridge**.
- **Coronavirus particles** are airborne particles/aerosols → you need a **particulate filter rated at least N95**, and preferably **P100**.

The pinkish/gray cartridges may be a **combination organic vapor + P100** cartridge, which would be good for both, but color alone isn’t reliable. Look for markings stamped on the cartridge such as **NIOSH**, **OV**, **P95/P100**, **N95**, etc. If it says **OV/P100**, that’s likely appropriate for both paint fumes and viral particles. If it only says **OV**, it helps fumes but not viruses. If it only says **P100/N95**, it helps particles but not paint vapors.

A few important points:

1. **Fit matters a lot.** Facial hair, gaps, or a poor seal can greatly reduce protection. Do a seal check each time you wear it.  
2. Many half-face respirators have an **exhalation valve**. That protects *you* well, but it doesn’t filter what you breathe out, so in some public places it may not meet “mask” rules unless covered with a surgical/procedure mask.  
3. Don’t wear old, dirty, wet, cracked, or hard-to-breathe-through cartridges. Organic vapor cartridges also “wear out” even if they look fine.  
4. In basements, use ventilation. Respirators do **not** protect against low oxygen or very high chemical exposures.

Given your wheezing around paint, it would be smart to get checked for asthma/reactive airway disease, especially if this is recurring. Stop working and get fresh air if you feel chest tightness, significant wheezing, dizziness, headache, or nausea.

Seek urgent care/911 for severe shortness of breath, chest pain, blue lips, confusion, or trouble speaking full sentences.

Best next step: take a photo of the cartridge label/model number or bring it to Sherwin-Williams and ask specifically: “Is this NIOSH-approved **OV/P100**?”

query: hi sorry if this is long im just getting myself worked up reading too much online. my daughter mia hannah lewis turned 1 yr on 20 march and since then i feel like every little temp makes me panic. in the last couple months when she had fever above 38 deg celsius we alternated between 5ml nurofen and 125mg empaped (paracetamol) suppository every 4 hours till temp broke, like thats what we were told before and it worked, but now with all the covid19 stuff i keep seeing posts saying dont use ibuprofen and then other posts saying its fine and i honestly dont know what to trust anymore. her card has nhs no 485 222 9173 and last time we saw dr priya nolan she didnt seem worried about the nurofen but that was before all this ibuprofen/corona talk started going round. i bought the nurofen from boots on high st in croydon and its the baby one with the syringe, 3 months plus, so i think dose is right for her weight but i keep second guessing myself. she is not boiling right this second but if she spikes again tonight do i just use paracetamol only, or is nurofen safe now regarding ibuprofen and covid19? i dont want to make anything worse if it was coronavirus, but also when her temp goes up she gets miserable and wont sleep and i end up checking her every 10 mins. sorry rambling, just need clear advice what to do if fever above 38 comes back, same 5ml nurofen alternating with the 125mg empaped every 4 hours till temp broke, or avoid nurofen completely for now?
pii_units: boots on high st in croydon
target_response:
Hi — I completely understand why you’re anxious. There was a lot of confusing information online early in COVID about ibuprofen, but current NHS/medical advice is that **ibuprofen (Nurofen) can be used in children with fever, including if COVID is possible**, as long as there’s no usual reason to avoid it.

For Mia, if a fever over 38°C comes back tonight: **treat how she is, not just the number**. If she’s drinking, responsive and not too uncomfortable, you don’t have to give medicine just to “break” the temperature. If she’s miserable or not sleeping, you can use:

- **Paracetamol**: 125 mg suppository is a typical dose for a 1-year-old, but follow the packet/weight guidance. Usually every **4–6 hours**, maximum **4 doses in 24 hours**.  
- **Ibuprofen/Nurofen for Children**: if it is the usual 100 mg/5 ml strength, **5 ml** is commonly the 1–3 year dose, usually every **6–8 hours**, maximum **3 doses in 24 hours**.

I would **not routinely alternate them every 4 hours until the temperature breaks**, because it’s easy to lose track and accidentally exceed the daily maximum. A safer approach is: start with one medicine for discomfort; if she’s still distressed before the next dose is due, you can consider the other, keeping a written note of time/dose.

Avoid ibuprofen if Mia is **dehydrated**, vomiting a lot, has significant diarrhoea, known kidney problems, stomach bleeding/ulcers, has had wheeze/asthma worsened by ibuprofen, or if you suspect **chickenpox**.

Also help with fluids, keeping her lightly dressed, and checking her periodically rather than every 10 minutes if she’s breathing comfortably.

Seek urgent help/111 or emergency care if she has trouble breathing, blue lips, is floppy or hard to wake, has a non-blanching rash, a seizure, stiff neck, persistent vomiting, very few wet nappies/no urine for 8–12 hours, is inconsolable, or fever lasts **5 days or more** or she seems to be getting worse.

You’re not rambling — you’re being a careful mum. The Nurofen itself does not need to be avoided just because of COVID concerns.

query: good afternoon, my name is janelle morris and i recently visited a client, melissa hayes, at her house for work and now im kinda sitting here overthinking everything. her husband dr ben hayes works in the same practice as a confirmed covid case, its northside family practice, and she told me that like after i was already there which is why im anxious now. i was only there for a regular client visit, not super long, but we were in the kitchen talking and i did touch the table, chair, my folder, all that, and i keep replaying it. two days after the visit i fell ill, gradually getting a sore throat, fever and feeling tired. its not like i woke up dying or anything, it just sort of crept in and now i feel run down and weird and im worried bc the timing seems too close to ignore. i dont know if im being dramatic or if this is exactly when people are supposed to get checked. i have been trying to stay in my room away from my kids and wiping stuff down but its hard and i dont want to scare everybody either. my work keeps asking if im coming in and i dont wanna be that person who spreads it if this is covid. if you need to reach me my cell is 312-555-9048 and my email is janelle.morris77@proton.me but really i just need to know, should i get tested? like do i call ahead somewhere, urgent care, health dept, or just stay home unless it gets worse? i have a sore throat, fever and tired feeling, no other big details, but the confirmed case connection has me nervous.
target_response:
Good afternoon, Janelle — I understand why this is making you anxious, especially since you learned about the possible COVID connection only after the visit and now you have symptoms.

Based on what you described, **yes, you should get tested for COVID**. A sore throat, fever, and fatigue can be COVID, but they can also be flu, RSV, strep throat, or another viral illness. Symptoms starting **2 days after an exposure is possible** for COVID, though the connection you described is indirect unless Melissa or her husband were symptomatic or tested positive. Still, because you are ill and you work with clients, testing is the responsible next step.

**What to do now:**
1. **Stay home from work for now** and let them know you have fever/respiratory symptoms and are getting tested.
2. **Take a COVID test today** if you have access to a home rapid test. If it is negative, repeat it in 24–48 hours, especially if symptoms continue.
3. If you cannot test at home, **call your primary care office, a pharmacy testing site, urgent care, or your local health department** to ask about testing. It’s best to **call ahead** before going in, so they can tell you where to go and whether to wear a mask/come through a specific entrance.
4. Continue limiting close contact with your kids as much as practical: mask if you’re around them, wash hands often, don’t share cups/utensils, improve ventilation, and wipe high-touch surfaces.

For symptom care: rest, fluids, acetaminophen or ibuprofen for fever/aches if you can take them safely, and monitor your temperature.

**Seek urgent care now** if you develop trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, confusion, bluish lips/face, severe weakness, dehydration, fever that is very high or not improving, or if you have risk factors like pregnancy, significant lung/heart disease, immune suppression, or uncontrolled medical conditions.

In general, stay home until your fever is gone for at least 24 hours without fever-reducing medicine and your symptoms are improving; if COVID-positive, follow your local/current isolation guidance and take extra precautions around others for several more days.

query:
hi, this is mike dellaro and im prob overthinking but i heard ace inhibitors might exacerbate coronavirus. i also heard nsaids might do the same. does this mean any common meds could have an unexpected effect during coronavirus? like im not trying to be dramatic but every time i read one thing online it sends me into a whole spiral and then another article says the opposite and now im just sitting here staring at the bottles in the bathroom like ok what is even safe anymore. i called dr. priya nanda’s office but they were slammed and said to send a portal msg, but the portal keeps timing out and im not sure if this is something urgent or just me being nervous. im seen at penn family medicine spruce st if that matters, and my insurance is aetna #W73920411, but honestly im asking in plain english bc i dont wanna make an appt just to be told “dont google stuff.”

so with the ace inhibitors thing, is that actually a real concern or just early rumor? and same with nsaids, like if someone gets fever or aches during coronavirus are they supposed to avoid them completely, or is it only certain people, or only if symptoms are bad? i get that nobody can give a perfect answer but im trying to understand if normal everyday meds can randomly make covid worse, or interact with the virus somehow, or if thats not really how it works. it feels like every common thing in the cabinet is suddenly suspicious and i dont know what to believe. also if someone already takes a med every day, are they supposed to stop it during coronavirus or would stopping be worse? sorry this is long, im just anxious and trying not to do something dumb based off internet rumors.
target_response:
Hi Mike — I’m glad you asked, and you’re not being dramatic. Early in COVID there were a lot of theoretical concerns circulating online, and some were amplified before good evidence existed.

**ACE inhibitors/ARBs:** The concern was based on how the virus enters cells through the ACE2 receptor, but clinical studies since then have **not shown that ACE inhibitors or ARBs make COVID worse**. Major cardiology and medical societies recommend that people **continue them if they were prescribed**. Stopping blood pressure or heart medications suddenly can be riskier than any theoretical COVID concern. So if you take something like lisinopril, enalapril, benazepril, losartan, valsartan, etc., don’t stop it unless Dr. Nanda or another clinician specifically tells you to.

**NSAIDs like ibuprofen/naproxen:** Early rumors suggested they might worsen COVID, but evidence has **not confirmed that NSAIDs worsen COVID outcomes**. For fever or aches, many clinicians suggest **acetaminophen/Tylenol first** because it’s generally easier on the stomach, kidneys, and blood pressure. But ibuprofen or naproxen can be reasonable for many people if they normally tolerate them. Avoid or check first if you have kidney disease, stomach ulcers/bleeding, are on blood thinners, have significant heart failure, uncontrolled high blood pressure, or have been told to avoid NSAIDs.

**Could everyday meds unexpectedly affect COVID?** In general, routine medications don’t “randomly” make COVID worse. Some medications can affect immunity or interact with specific COVID treatments like Paxlovid, but that’s handled case-by-case. The safest rule is: **do not stop any daily medication on your own because of COVID rumors.** If you develop COVID and are prescribed treatment, tell the clinician/pharmacist everything you take, including supplements.

This doesn’t sound urgent if you’re currently just trying to sort out medication safety. If you develop COVID symptoms, you can use acetaminophen for fever/aches, stay hydrated, and contact Penn Family Medicine about testing/treatment options, especially if you’re higher risk.

Seek urgent care now for trouble breathing, chest pain, blue lips, confusion, severe dehydration, fainting, or oxygen levels persistently below 92% if you monitor them.

And honestly: “don’t Google” isn’t enough — but do try to use CDC, FDA, or your health system’s guidance rather than random articles.

query: sore throat, ear ache, headache and a light cough. do i need to worry? its me maya ellis and im prob overthinking but i hate when its throat plus ear at the same time bc then i start thinking its strep or an ear infection or something worse. i woke up with it and its just been annoying all day, not like im gasping or anything, just scratchy sore throat and the ear ache is kinda dull and the headache is sitting behind my eyes a bit. the cough is light, like not deep in my chest, just every once in awhile, but still it makes me nervous bc everyone at work has been sick lately and i dont wanna be the person spreading stuff around. i tried drinking tea and water and i took regular stuff from the cabinet but idk if im supposed to wait it out or go in. i was gonna call northside family clinic but i always feel dumb calling if its just a cold, and then if i dont call i worry im ignoring something. if someone needs to reach me my cell is 267-555-0138 but honestly im just trying to figure out if this is like urgent care tonight or just rest, fluids, maybe test at home and see. no big scary symptoms that i can tell, just uncomfortable and tired and kinda anxious about the ear part. also if its strep would the cough be light like this or not usually? sorry im rambling, i just want to know what signs mean i should worry vs just give it a day or two.
target_response:
Hi Maya — you’re not being dumb or “overthinking.” Throat + ear pain can feel alarming, but the pattern you describe — scratchy sore throat, dull ear ache, headache behind the eyes, light occasional cough, and coworkers sick — most commonly fits a viral upper respiratory infection/cold, COVID/flu, or sinus/nasal congestion causing pressure and referred ear pain through the Eustachian tube. A true ear infection is possible, but usually the ear pain becomes more intense, often with fever, reduced hearing, drainage, or feeling quite ill.

Strep throat is **less likely when there’s a cough**, runny/stuffy nose, or hoarseness, but it’s not impossible. Strep more typically causes sudden significant throat pain, fever, swollen/tender neck glands, no cough, and sometimes white patches on the tonsils.

For tonight, if you’re breathing fine and don’t have high fever or severe symptoms, it’s reasonable to **wait it out with supportive care**:
- Do a home COVID test now or tomorrow morning; repeat in 24–48 hours if negative and symptoms continue.
- Rest, fluids, warm tea/honey, salt-water gargles, throat lozenges.
- Acetaminophen or ibuprofen can help headache/throat/ear pain if you can take them safely. Be careful not to double up on acetaminophen if your “cabinet meds” are combo cold medicines.
- Saline nasal spray, steam/humidifier, or a gentle decongestant may help ear pressure if you’re congested.

Because people at work are sick, I’d mask and avoid close contact until you know more, especially if you develop fever or test positive.

**Go to urgent care tonight** if you develop trouble breathing, trouble swallowing saliva/drooling, severe one-sided throat pain or neck swelling, stiff neck, confusion, chest pain, dehydration, a fever over 103°F, severe/worsening headache with vision changes, or severe ear pain with drainage or sudden hearing loss.

Otherwise, call Northside Family Clinic tomorrow or the next day if symptoms worsen, fever develops, ear pain persists/worsens beyond 24–48 hours, or sore throat is still significant after 2–3 days — they can decide if you need a strep test or ear exam.

query: my fiancé marcus ellis has been coughing uncontrollably every night for the past 4 months and i mean every single night, like we barely sleep anymore and im starting to get scared bc it sounds so bad sometimes. he is 23 years old, dob 08/14/2002, 5'9", about 140 lbs., and has been told by his dr. rebecca patel that he has walking pneumonia. his chart thing from the visit says MRN HMC-774218 if that matters. he has been prescribed antibiotics, cough syrup, allergy medicine, mucinex, nasal sprays, and inhalers and although they suppress the coughing at times he still does not seem to have gotten rid of the coughing. we picked up most of it from cvs on oak ave and he did take it, not perfectly at the exact same time every day maybe, but he didnt just ignore it either. his coughing is accompanied with phlegm and has also caused him to throw up due to the extremity, like he coughs so hard he gags and then throws up and then hes exhausted. he does smoke cigars as well, not cigarettes really, but cigars and i keep telling him it prob isnt helping but he says its not that much and gets annoyed when i bring it up. what is this!? could walking pneumonia last this long even after all these meds?? could it be asthma or allergies or something worse like his lungs are damaged? he doesnt have a fever that i know of but the nighttime cough is insane and sometimes he has to sit up or go to the bathroom to spit out phlegm. i just feel like 4 months is way too long and i dont want them to keep handing him random stuff if nobody is figuring out why hes still coughing like this.
target_response:
I’m sorry you and Marcus are going through this — a nightly, uncontrollable cough for **4 months** with phlegm and vomiting from coughing is exhausting and understandably scary. Also, for privacy, you don’t need to share details like MRN/DOB in messages like this.

You’re right: **4 months is too long to assume this is just “walking pneumonia.”** After pneumonia, a cough can linger for several weeks, but persistent nightly cough despite antibiotics/inhalers/allergy meds needs a more focused re-evaluation rather than just more medications.

Common possibilities include:

- **Cough-variant asthma or airway inflammation** after infection — often worse at night and may improve only partially with inhalers if technique/dosing isn’t right.
- **Post-nasal drip/allergies/sinus irritation** — mucus draining at night can cause phlegmy coughing and gagging.
- **GERD/reflux** — can cause severe nighttime cough even without heartburn.
- **Ongoing irritation from cigar smoke** — even “not much” can keep airways inflamed and prevent healing.
- **Pertussis/whooping cough or atypical infection**, especially with coughing fits causing vomiting.
- Less commonly: persistent pneumonia, bronchiectasis, TB, immune issues, or another lung condition.

He should contact Dr. Patel and ask for a **prompt follow-up this week**, or consider a pulmonology referral. Reasonable next steps would include: a **repeat chest X-ray** if not done recently, possibly **CT chest** if abnormal or symptoms persist, **spirometry/pulmonary function testing** for asthma, oxygen level check, review of inhaler technique, and possibly sputum testing or testing for pertussis/other infections depending on history.

For now: he should **stop cigars completely** for at least several weeks — this is not a small factor. Sleep propped up, use a humidifier, hydrate, avoid smoke/fragrances, and avoid eating 2–3 hours before bed in case reflux is contributing.

Seek urgent care/ER now if he has **trouble breathing, chest pain, coughing blood, blue lips, fainting, high fever, oxygen level <92%, severe weakness/dehydration from vomiting, or rapid worsening**. Otherwise, this deserves a structured workup, not just repeated “try this” treatments.

query: hi, this is melissa garrity, i have been recently diagnosed w the flu (nose swab test done) at northside family care and i am trying not to be dramatic but i am kinda spiraling bc every single time i get some viral thing it feels like it turns into my chest. i am 34 years old- ex-smoker (quit completely for 7 years now) however each year i get "walking pneumonia" at least once. i'm almost certain i've got it again now. like i know the flu can make you cough and feel awful and all that, but my chest is getting that familiar heavy/tight gross feeling and i keep thinking ok here we go again, same thing as every winter basically. i already picked up the flu meds they gave me at cvs on 14th st and ive been doing fluids, resting, trying to sleep propped up, humidifier, all the boring stuff, but i dont know if theres anything real i can do to stop this from becoming pneumonia again or stop getting pneumonia every year?? it just seems weird that im not smoking anymore and havent for 7 years but this keeps happening. i dont vape either. i get anxious bc i can usually tell when it moves from just flu/cold junk into the walking pneumonia feeling, but then i also dont wanna run back in too early and be told its just the flu and go home. is there like a preventative thing, vaccine, inhaler, breathing treatment, vitamins, lung check, anything?? should i be asking for a chest xray right away when i feel it starting or wait for fever/sob/worse cough? im just tired of doing this yearly and missing work and feeling like my lungs are trash even tho i quit so long ago.
target_response:
Hi Melissa — I’m really glad you reached out. You’re not being dramatic; given your history of yearly “walking pneumonia” and that familiar chest heaviness/tightness, it makes sense that the flu would make you anxious.

A few key points: influenza itself can cause a deep cough, chest burning/tightness, fatigue, and heaviness for several days. But it can also set people up for bronchitis, asthma-like airway inflammation, or pneumonia — so the pattern you’re describing is worth taking seriously.

**For right now:** keep taking the flu medication as prescribed, fluids/rest/humidifier/propping up are all appropriate. If you have access to a pulse oximeter, check your oxygen level a few times daily while resting. I’d recommend calling Northside Family Care for guidance **sooner rather than later** if your chest tightness is increasing, your cough is worsening, or you feel this is following your usual pneumonia pattern. A chest X-ray is not always needed on day 1 of flu symptoms, but it *is* reasonable if you have persistent fever, shortness of breath, low oxygen, chest pain with breathing, or a “worse after briefly improving” pattern.

**Go to urgent care/ER now** if you have trouble breathing, oxygen under ~94%, blue lips, confusion, severe chest pain, coughing blood, dehydration, or fever that won’t come down / is very high.

Longer term, getting “pneumonia” every winter at age 34 — especially after quitting smoking 7 years ago and not vaping — deserves a deeper look. Sometimes what feels like “walking pneumonia” is recurrent bronchitis, reactive airways/asthma, post-viral wheezing, allergies/postnasal drip, reflux/aspiration, or less commonly immune issues or bronchiectasis. Useful next steps to ask about after you’re through the acute flu:

- **Spirometry/lung function testing** to look for asthma/reactive airway disease  
- Review whether prior episodes were confirmed by **chest X-ray**  
- Consider labs like CBC and immunoglobulins if infections are truly recurrent  
- Pulmonology referral or CT chest if pneumonia is repeatedly confirmed, especially in the same area  
- Vaccine review: yearly flu, COVID boosters, and whether you qualify for pneumococcal vaccination

Inhalers/nebulizers can help if there’s wheezing or airway spasm, but they should be guided by an exam. Vitamins generally don’t prevent pneumonia unless you’re deficient.

query: hi i was confirmed to have covid-19, i self-isolated for 14 days and i really did try to do it right like stayed in my room, wiped the door handles, wore a mask even going to the bathroom, all that stuff, but now my concern is with my 1 year 6 months son nabi who has been caughing. ? im marisol vega and i can be reached at 347-555-0196 if someone needs to call, but mostly i just need to know what to watch for bc im scared i maybe gave it to him even tho i kept away from him as much as i could. his cough is not constant every second but enough that i hear it and then i panic, and he is little so he cant tell me if his chest hurts or if he feels weird. he is still playing some and drinking but then he coughs and i start thinking the worst. i dont know if i should bring him in or wait, bc i dont want to expose him more either. when i got checked it was at elmhurst hospital urgent care and they just told me isolate and watch symptoms, and i finished the 14 days, but i dont understand if that means im totally safe around him now or not. i also dont know if toddlers need testing or if they just say stay home unless breathing is bad. i have tylenol from cvs on 37th ave but i dont wanna just give stuff wrong or cover up something. no one is really explaining what to do with a baby/toddler after a parent had confirmed covid-19. should i keep him away from me still, should his dad take him, what signs mean emergency? the caughing is making me so nervous.
target_response:
Hi Marisol — I can hear how worried you are, and you did a lot of the right things to protect Nabi. Even with careful isolation, household spread can still happen, so please don’t blame yourself.

For you: after COVID, you are generally considered much less contagious once **at least 10 days** have passed from symptom onset/positive test **and** you’ve had **no fever for 24 hours without fever medicine** and symptoms are improving. Since you completed 14 days, if you meet those criteria, it’s reasonable to be around him again, but keep using good handwashing and consider a mask if you’re still coughing.

For Nabi: many toddlers with COVID or other viruses have mild symptoms—cough, runny nose, low fever—and continue to play and drink. Since he is **still playing some and drinking**, that is reassuring. However, because he is 18 months old and had a known exposure, I’d recommend you **call his pediatrician today** for guidance on whether he should be tested and whether they want to examine him. Many offices can do a telehealth visit first to avoid unnecessary exposure.

What you can do at home:
- Encourage fluids; watch wet diapers/urination.
- Use a humidifier or steamy bathroom for cough congestion.
- Saline drops/spray and gentle suction if his nose is stuffy.
- **Do not use over-the-counter cough/cold medicines** at his age unless his doctor specifically says so.
- Tylenol is okay for fever/discomfort, but dosing must be based on his weight—ask his pediatrician or pharmacist for the correct dose.

Seek urgent care/ER now if you notice: fast or hard breathing, ribs pulling in, nostrils flaring, grunting, blue/gray lips, unusual sleepiness or limpness, dehydration signs (very few wet diapers, no tears, dry mouth), fever in a child who looks very ill, persistent fever, repeated vomiting, or if you feel something is seriously wrong.

If his dad is well and able to help, that’s okay, but if he’s already in the same household, the exposure may have already occurred. The key now is monitoring Nabi closely and connecting with his pediatrician.

query: hi my name is nandi khumalo and i am experiencing a sore throat, fever, headache and coughing. i have a bit of a runny nose, short breath but only from time to time, like not the whole day but then i suddenly feel like i must sit down and breathe slowly and then it passes. i have traveled on the mcs cruise to mozambique 13-16 march 2020 and since coming back i keep thinking maybe i should not have gone out or been around people, because there was lots of ppl coughing on the ship and in the queues and at the buffet, and now every little thing is making me panic. i dont know if this is just normal flu or sinus or from the aircon on the boat but the fever and headache is making me worried, and the cough is not nice especially at night when i lie down. i think it might be necessary to do a covid-19? i am not trying to overreact but also i dont want to go to work or see family if i can make someone sick, especially my gran. i tried calling but the lines are busy and i dont know if i must just come in or if i need to phone first. my cell is 082-555-0196 if someone can pls tell me what to do, because i dont want to sit in a waiting room and infect people if this is covid. the only travel paper i have here says booking msc-47k2q9, if that helps check the cruise dates or whatever. should i self isolate now and for how long, and where do i go for the test?
target_response:
Hi Nandi — you are not overreacting. With fever, sore throat, headache, cough, runny nose and episodes of shortness of breath after being on a crowded cruise, it is sensible to treat this as a possible contagious viral infection, including COVID-19, until proven otherwise.

Please **self-isolate now**: do not go to work, avoid visitors, and stay away from your gran or anyone elderly/medically vulnerable. Stay in one room if possible, use a separate bathroom if available, wear a mask if you must be near others, wash hands often, and don’t share cups/towels.

Because you have fever and cough plus travel/crowd exposure, you should **arrange COVID testing**, but **phone first**—do not sit in a waiting room. Contact your GP/clinic or your local COVID hotline/testing centre for instructions. If you are in South Africa, you can try the national COVID hotline **0800 029 999** or WhatsApp **0600 123 456**. I can’t phone you from here, but when you call, mention the cruise dates and your symptoms; the booking reference is less important than the exposure details.

For now: rest, drink fluids, use **paracetamol** for fever/headache as directed on the packet, try honey/lemon or lozenges for cough, saline spray/steam for congestion, and sleep slightly propped up to reduce night coughing.

The “short breath” episodes could be from the infection, anxiety/panic, or bronchospasm—but please take them seriously. **Seek urgent medical care immediately** if shortness of breath becomes persistent or occurs at rest, you have chest pain, blue lips, confusion, fainting, severe weakness, worsening fever, coughing blood, or you cannot keep fluids down.

Isolation length depends on test results and local guidance. As a safe rule: isolate until you have medical advice/test results; if COVID is confirmed or strongly suspected, continue isolation for at least **10 days from symptom onset** and until you are fever-free for 24 hours and improving. If advised under travel-exposure rules, you may be asked to isolate for **14 days**.

You’re doing the right thing by protecting your family and others.

query: hi im maria lopez and sorry this is prob a dumb question but does a virus cause lesions dry and oily nose like at same time?? i keep trying to google it and then i scare myself and it says 500 diff things so now im here. my nose is weirdly dry on the inside and around the edges but also oily on top like shiny, and i have these little lesions or sore spots and idk if thats from a virus or from touching it too much or from the weather or what. i didnt wanna make it sound dramatic when i called but im kinda anxious bc it looks gross and feels tight and then greasy somehow. my dob is 04/18/1989 if that matters for the chart, and i think my mrn is MRN ML-33719 from the last time i went in. i saw dr. kevin shah once for a rash before but this feels diff and i dont know if i should go back or just wait it out. the front desk at riverbend family clinic said i could send a msg first bc appts are backed up and i dont wanna waste a slot if its just a virus thing. i have not been putting anything crazy on it besides washing my face and i did use a little moisturizer but then it seemed more oily so i stopped. i also dont know if lesions means like open sores or just red patches?? mine are small but annoying and im checking them every hour which prob makes it worse. if i need something otc i can grab it from CVS on 14th ave later, but i dont wanna put steroid cream or antibiotic stuff on my nose if thats bad. mostly asking can a virus do this, dry and oily nose with lesions, or is that more allergy/skin thing?
target_response:
Hi Maria — not a dumb question at all. The nose can feel confusing because the skin there has oil glands, while the edges/inside can get very dry and irritated at the same time.

A virus *can* cause nose sores, especially cold-sore virus/HSV, which often looks like clusters of tiny painful blisters that crust over, sometimes with burning/tingling. But what you’re describing—dry/tight edges or inside of the nose plus shiny/oily top and small sore spots—more commonly fits skin irritation or inflammation, such as:

- **Irritant/contact dermatitis** from frequent washing, rubbing, checking/touching, tissues, weather changes, or products  
- **Seborrheic dermatitis**, which can cause greasy/shiny skin with redness/flaking around the nose  
- **Nasal vestibulitis**, irritation/infection just inside the nostril, often from rubbing/picking/blowing; can cause tender little sores or crusting  
- Less commonly: acne/rosacea, allergies, impetigo, or HSV

“Lesions” just means an abnormal spot on the skin—it can be a red patch, bump, sore, blister, crack, or open area.

For now, I’d keep it simple for 5–7 days:
1. **Stop checking/touching as much as possible**—this really can keep it inflamed.  
2. Wash with a **gentle, fragrance-free cleanser** once daily; avoid scrubs, acids/retinoids, alcohol toners.  
3. Apply a thin layer of **plain petroleum jelly/Aquaphor** to the dry edges and just at the nostril entrance. Don’t pack it deep inside.  
4. Use a **light, non-comedogenic moisturizer** on the outside skin even if it looks oily—over-drying can make oiliness worse.  
5. I’d avoid steroid cream or antibiotic ointment unless a clinician recommends it, especially on/inside the nose.

You should schedule a visit with Dr. Shah or any available clinician if it’s not improving in a week, keeps recurring, becomes very painful, or has crusting/pus—sometimes a prescription like mupirocin is needed.

Seek urgent care sooner if you develop fever, rapidly spreading redness/swelling, severe tenderness of the nose tip, pus/honey-colored crusting, swelling near the eye, or red streaking. Taking a clear photo today can help track changes.

query: my name is tanya mills dob 11/03/1989 and i have been sick for going on four weeks now. it keeps changing each week from a stuff nose wear i cant breathe at all to my throat hurting and my chest hurting when i breathe or talk. like one week its all in my head and nose and im up all night blowing it and then the next its my throat so bad i dont even wanna swallow and now its my chest hurting if i take a deep breath or even talk too much. im constantly falling asleep like i sit down for 5 min and im out, and i keep having a fever on and off all the time. not crazy high every sec but it keeps coming back and then i get sweaty and gross and then cold again. i went to mercy urgent care on 5th ave a week ago n they said i had the beginning of pneumonia. he only gave me a zpack and some cough medicine, which i picked up at cvs on broad st, and i did what they told me and took it how it said. the thing is i feel worse not better at all....what should i do? im trying not to be dramatic but im scared bc i thought antibiotics were supposed to help and i still feel like crap. i cant keep missing work and i feel like no one is really listening bc my lungs werent like totally horrible yet or whatever. should i go back to urgent care or go to the er or call someone first? im worried this is turning into worse pneumonia or something else, and breathing/talking hurting is freaking me out. also if the zpack is done and im still sick do i need different meds or another xray or what, i just dont know what is normal after 4 weeks.
target_response:
Hi Tanya — I’m sorry you’ve been dealing with this for almost a month. You’re not being dramatic. Ongoing on-and-off fevers, marked fatigue/sleepiness, and chest pain with deep breathing or talking **after completing a Z-pack** are reasons to be re-evaluated promptly.

A Z-pack can help some causes of pneumonia, but it doesn’t cover every bacteria, and sometimes what starts as a viral illness can turn into a bacterial infection or cause inflammation around the lungs (“pleurisy”). Worsening symptoms can also mean the pneumonia is progressing, there’s fluid around the lung, asthma/bronchospasm, COVID/flu/RSV, or less commonly something more serious like a blood clot. The key is that you need a fresh exam and objective measurements.

**What I’d recommend:**  
- **Call your primary care office today** and ask for a same-day sick visit.  
- If they can’t see you today, **go back to urgent care today** or another urgent care that can do a chest X-ray and check your oxygen level.  
- Given your chest pain with breathing/talking, recurrent fever, and feeling worse after antibiotics, it would be reasonable to go to the **ER instead** if symptoms are significant or worsening.

You likely need: vital signs including oxygen saturation, lung exam, possibly a **repeat chest X-ray**, and sometimes bloodwork and testing for COVID/flu/RSV/strep. They can decide if you need a different antibiotic, inhaler/nebulizer treatment, steroids, or other testing.

**Go to the ER now / call 911** if you have any of these: trouble breathing at rest, blue lips, oxygen level under 92% if you have a pulse ox, chest pressure/heaviness, coughing blood, fainting/confusion, fever over 103°F, severe weakness/dehydration, one-sided leg swelling/pain, or chest pain that is severe or worsening.

In the meantime: rest, fluids, acetaminophen/ibuprofen if you can take them safely, and avoid smoking/vaping. Don’t start leftover antibiotics or repeat a Z-pack without being seen. Antibiotics usually start helping within 48–72 hours; feeling worse a week later is **not something to just wait out**.

query: last weekend (13-15 march) i was at a festival, riverfront music fest, and i was there pretty much all 3 days bc i already had the pass and had taken off work and didnt think it was gonna be a thing yet. my name is mara ellis, dob 07/09/1991, and im prob overthinking but im kind of spiraling now. the festival was at pier 84, 555 12th ave, new york, ny and we were mostly standing near the food trucks / beer tent area for hours, like not smashed shoulder to shoulder the whole time but def close enough to people and lines and bathrooms and all that. and today the festival organizers confirmed that someone arrived on the 15th who was tested positive for covid-19. i was in the same area and for a few hours where this person was, so now im replaying every little cough i heard and every time i touched my phone and then ate fries, gross i know. i dont have a fever right now, or at least last time i checked it was normal, but i feel like my throat is kinda weird maybe just anxiety? i live with my bf and he keeps saying calm down but i dont wanna be the person who ignores it and then gets somebody else sick. i have Blue Shield PPO #BS-4481092 if that matters for where i can go, and my email is mara.ellis91@gmail.com if someone needs to send info, but mostly i just need to know should i get tested? or only quarantine unless symptoms show up? also if i was there 13-15 march and this person arrived on the 15th does the timing make it less scary or still a real exposure?
target_response:
Hi Mara — I’m glad you reached out. It’s very understandable to feel anxious after hearing that someone at the same festival tested positive, especially when you were there for long periods around food lines, bathrooms, and crowds.

The timing matters: if the person only arrived on March 15, then March 13–14 would not be exposure to *that* individual. March 15 could still represent a real exposure if you were near them or in shared crowded areas for a prolonged time. Outdoor settings are generally lower risk than indoor spaces, but lines, close conversations, bathrooms, and touching shared surfaces can increase risk.

COVID symptoms typically appear about 2–14 days after exposure, most often around days 3–5. A “weird throat” can absolutely be from anxiety, dry air, allergies, or early infection — so it’s worth monitoring without assuming the worst.

What I’d recommend now:
- Count March 15 as your last possible exposure date.
- Limit close contact with others, especially anyone high-risk.
- Around your boyfriend: improve ventilation, avoid sharing cups/utensils, wash hands often, and consider masking indoors if possible until you know more.
- Check your temperature once or twice daily and watch for fever, cough, sore throat, congestion, fatigue, body aches, headache, shortness of breath, or loss of taste/smell.
- Testing is reasonable. Ideally test about 5 days after exposure, or sooner if symptoms develop. A test done too early can be falsely negative, so if you test early and symptoms appear later, repeat testing is appropriate.
- If you test positive, isolate and follow current local health guidance.

Seek urgent care or emergency help if you develop trouble breathing, persistent chest pain/pressure, confusion, fainting, bluish lips/face, severe weakness, dehydration, or a high fever that won’t come down.

Your Blue Shield PPO may help with where you can go, but for testing you can also check local pharmacy sites, urgent cares, your primary care office, or NYC/public health testing resources. Also, try not to share insurance numbers, DOB, or email in open messages unless you’re in a secure medical portal.

Bottom line: this was a possible exposure, not a certainty. Taking precautions and testing at the right time is the responsible next step.

query: i believe i might have covid 19 symtoms and im kinda stressing bc i dont know if im overreacting or if i should be doing something asap. my name is marisa elliot, dob 08/14/1991, and im at 1187 maple ave apt 3b, pittsburgh pa right now by myself, so im trying to figure out if it's possible to get testing done at home to confirm? currently i have soar throat, started last night around 19:30, like it came on pretty fast while i was just sitting on the couch and then i kept swallowing and it felt scratchy and weird. i dont have a ride today and i really dont wanna sit in an urgent care waiting room if this is covid and get other ppl sick or catch something else. i tried looking online but every site says something different and some say go in, some say stay home, some say call first, so idk. my phone is 412-555-0198 if someone has to call me instead of msg. i have upmc health plan #UPM-4472019 but im not even sure if they cover someone coming to the house or if thats only for older ppl. i work around ppl all day and im worried bc i was there yesterday before the throat thing started and now im thinking what if i exposed everyone. i dont feel horrible horrible, just worried and my throat is sore and i keep checking myself. can a nurse or lab person come out to swab me or do i need to pick up a kit somewhere, and if i do that should i be isolating already while i wait?
target_response:
Hi Marisa — I understand why this feels stressful, especially being home by yourself and working around other people. A sore throat that came on quickly can be COVID, but it can also be a common cold, flu, strep throat, allergies/irritation, or reflux. You’re not overreacting by wanting to test and avoid exposing others.

**What to do now:**
1. **Start isolating/limiting contact now** while you’re symptomatic. If you must be around others, wear a well-fitting mask.
2. **Use a home rapid COVID test if you can get one delivered** (pharmacy delivery, Instacart/DoorDash, or asking someone to drop it at your door). Since symptoms started last night, a test today may be negative early—if negative, **repeat in 48 hours**.
3. **At-home PCR or mobile swab services** may exist locally, but availability/coverage varies. Your best next step is to call your **UPMC Health Plan member services** or your PCP’s office/nurse line and ask specifically about “home COVID testing” or “mobile lab collection.” This chat can’t arrange a call or dispatch someone directly.
4. If you do need in-person testing, **call first** before urgent care/pharmacy testing and wear a mask the entire time.

**While waiting:** rest, fluids, warm tea/honey, throat lozenges, salt-water gargles, and acetaminophen or ibuprofen if you can take them safely.

**Work/exposure:** Because COVID can be contagious before symptoms, it’s reasonable to let your supervisor know you developed symptoms and are testing. If you test positive, follow workplace policy and current guidance: stay home until symptoms are improving and you’ve been fever-free for 24 hours without fever-reducing meds, then use extra precautions/masking for about 5 days.

**Seek urgent care now or call 911** if you develop trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, blue/gray lips, confusion, fainting, severe dehydration, inability to swallow saliva, or a fever that is high/persistent. Also get checked for possible **strep** if you have fever, swollen tender neck glands, pus on tonsils, or no cough.

For now, testing at home + repeat testing is a very reasonable plan.

query: my dad robert ellis, dob 03/14/1958, last week was vomiting and had diarrhea now this week it has turned into runny nose and a cough...no fever....any chance it’s covid-19? i know everybody prob asks this now but im sitting here overthinking it bc he keeps saying hes “fine” and then he coughs again and im like ok but are you tho. last week it was all stomach stuff, like he couldnt keep much down and was running to the bathroom, then that kinda calmed down and now its the runny nose and cough thing so idk if that means its just some bug moving around or if covid can start like that too. he has not had a fever at all when i check, i keep checking like too much probably, and he gets annoyed with me lol. i called dr. nina patel’s office but they just said watch symptoms unless worse, and mercy family clinic didnt have any same day spots when i tried earlier so im asking here before i drag him somewhere and expose him to more germs or whatever. he gets his meds at CVS on 9th ave and i was going to ask the pharmacist too but the line was long and he wanted to go home. nobody else in the house is sick right now, but im worried bc i have to be around him and i dont wanna bring anything to work or get my kids sick either. should he isolate like it is covid even with no fever? and is the vomiting/diarrhea from last week related to this week nose/cough or probably separate? just dont wanna miss something bc hes stubborn and acts like doctors are only for emergencies.
target_response:
I understand why you’re worried—especially when he’s minimizing it and you’re trying to protect your household and work. Yes, COVID-19 is **possible even without a fever**. COVID can cause runny nose/cough and can also start with GI symptoms like vomiting and diarrhea, though a “stomach bug” followed by a separate cold virus is also very possible.

Given his age, I’d treat this as potentially contagious until you know more:

1. **Test for COVID now** with a home rapid test if you have one. If it’s negative, repeat in **24–48 hours**, because early tests can miss it. If you can access PCR testing through a clinic/pharmacy, that’s another option.
2. While waiting/if symptoms continue: have him **stay home**, avoid close contact, and wear a **well-fitting mask** around others. You should mask around him too, wash hands often, and improve ventilation if possible.
3. If he tests positive, call Dr. Patel’s office promptly because older adults may qualify for antiviral treatment like Paxlovid, but it works best when started early and depends on his medications/kidney function.

For symptom care: encourage fluids/electrolytes, small bland meals, rest, saline spray, honey for cough if he can take it, and acetaminophen if aches occur. Avoid forcing cough/cold meds without checking, especially if he has high blood pressure, heart disease, prostate issues, glaucoma, or is on multiple medications—asking the CVS pharmacist is a good idea when it’s less busy or by phone.

Please seek urgent care or ER evaluation if he develops: trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, confusion, bluish lips, severe weakness, dehydration signs like dizziness, very little urine, inability to keep fluids down, bloody stool/vomit, oxygen saturation **below 92%** if you have a pulse ox, or fever that develops and persists/worsens.

If the diarrhea/vomiting has fully resolved and now it’s mainly cough/runny nose, it may be improving overall—but because COVID can look exactly like this, testing and temporary isolation are the safest approach. You’re not overreacting; you’re being appropriately cautious.

query: i was recently diagnosed with pneumonia and im still kind of in disbelief tbh. my name is dana kepler and i went to lakeview family medicine after trying to pretend it was just allergies or stress or whatever, and dr. nisha patel was the one who told me it was pneumonia. i had pneumonia when i was a baby but never since. i was shocked when i heard the diagnosis, like actually sat there staring at her because i dont get sick. the symptoms became apparent on march 28, first just feeling off and then the cough and wiped out feeling got worse. i began taking doxycycline monohydrate on monday april 3 and must take them for seven days, picked it up at cvs on 3rd ave and the pharmacist said take with water and not lay down right away so im trying to be good about that. i am mega healthy and have not been sick in years. i can t even remember the last time i was sick. this has really knocked me out. i have zero energy and very little appetite, like i walk from the bed to the kitchen and feel like i ran a race. im drinking water and forcing soup but its hard bc food just seems gross. how does pneumonia manifest in the lung and how does bacteria get in there? like did i breathe it in, was it already in my throat, did i miss some warning sign? i keep replaying the last week and wondering what i did wrong. how long will it be until i recover? i am really having a hard time with this. help!
target_response:
Hi Dana — I’m sorry you’re going through this. It’s very understandable to feel shocked, especially when you’re usually healthy and rarely get sick. Pneumonia can feel emotionally jarring because it can come on after what seems like “just allergies,” a cold, or stress, and then suddenly your body feels completely drained.

Pneumonia means there is inflammation and infection in the small air sacs of the lung. Those air sacs normally exchange oxygen, but when infected they can fill with fluid, mucus, and inflammatory cells — which is why people feel short of breath, cough, exhausted, achy, feverish, or like a short walk takes a huge amount of effort. Bacteria can get there in a few ways: tiny droplets breathed in, bacteria already living in the nose/throat slipping down into the lungs, or after a viral illness weakens local defenses. This does **not** mean you did something wrong or missed an obvious warning sign. Even very healthy people can get pneumonia.

Since your symptoms became apparent March 28 and you started doxycycline April 3, it’s common for recovery to be gradual. Many people start to notice some improvement within **48–72 hours after starting antibiotics**, but the cough and fatigue can last **2–4 weeks**, sometimes longer. Appetite often lags behind too. For now, think “small and steady”: frequent sips of fluids, broth/soup, smoothies, toast, crackers, rice, applesauce, or anything mild that you can tolerate. Rest is treatment — your body is spending a lot of energy healing lung tissue.

You’re doing the right thing with doxycycline: take it with a full glass of water and stay upright for at least 30 minutes. Also avoid taking it at the same time as calcium, iron, magnesium, antacids, or multivitamins, and be cautious with sun exposure.

Please seek urgent care now if you develop worsening shortness of breath, chest pain, blue lips, confusion, fainting, coughing blood, persistent high fever, oxygen levels under 92% if you’re checking, or you cannot keep fluids down. Also call Lakeview/Dr. Patel if you’re not clearly improving after 3 days on antibiotics or if symptoms worsen. You’re not failing at recovery — pneumonia is genuinely exhausting.

query: i am melissa harper dob 03/14/1981 and i am recovering from double bilateral pneumonia in hospital 3 days at st marys medical center and i m 2 weeks post discharge now and still kinda scared about how slow this feels. i m an rt so i knew i had to use incentive spirometer and nebulizers for 2 weeks and i have been doing it even when i dont want to, like sitting here counting breaths and trying not to panic when my chest hurts. dr priya nair told me to take it easy and i am trying but it is hard bc i feel like i should understand this better being an rt, but when its your own lungs it feels different and way more scary. i have to wear 02 at night only .5 to. 1 liter to keep sats above 90. during the day i occasionally need o2 too, not all day but if i get up and do too much or talk too long i feel it and i check and then i get anxious. i am on medical leave for 4 weeks and im worried bc i keep thinking ok its been 2 weeks why am i not breathing normal yet. is this going to get easier to breath soon or is this normal after pneumonia this bad. i am resting as much as possible. i don t have much choice rather rest than feel pain i. chest. i sleep propped up, drink water, do the nebulizers, try the spirometer, then feel wiped out. no one really tells you how long the weak feeling and air hunger hangs around and i keep wondering if i should push more walking or keep resting bc i dont want to set myself back.
target_response:
Melissa, I’m sorry you’re going through this. And you’re right: even as an RT, it feels very different when it’s *your* lungs and your own oxygen saturation on the screen. Two weeks after hospitalization for bilateral pneumonia is still very early in recovery, especially if you needed oxygen at discharge.

After a severe pneumonia, it’s common for breathing, chest discomfort with deep breaths/coughing, fatigue, and “air hunger” to improve slowly over several weeks. Many people don’t feel close to baseline for 4–8 weeks, and some fatigue can linger longer. The lungs may still be healing, airways can remain inflamed/reactive, and deconditioning happens quickly after even a short hospitalization.

That said, you should be seeing a gradual trend in the right direction—not necessarily day to day, but week to week. Needing 0.5–1 L at night and occasional daytime oxygen with exertion can be consistent with recovery, but it deserves close follow-up. I’d recommend checking in with Dr. Nair or your primary clinician this week or next, especially since you’re still needing oxygen. They may want to review your oxygen trends, lung exam, meds/nebulizers, and consider a follow-up chest X-ray around 6 weeks after pneumonia, or sooner if symptoms worsen.

For activity: don’t “push through” air hunger. Think gentle, paced rehab. Several short walks daily—2–5 minutes at a time if that’s all you can do—are better than one big effort. Use your oxygen as prescribed, pause if your saturation drops, and recover before resuming. Continue the incentive spirometer, but it shouldn’t wipe you out; shorter, more frequent sessions are fine. Rest is treatment right now.

Seek urgent care/ER if you develop: worsening shortness of breath at rest, oxygen saturations persistently below your instructed goal despite oxygen, increasing oxygen requirement, new/worsening chest pain especially sharp or one-sided, coughing blood, fever returning, confusion, blue lips, fainting, racing heart that won’t settle, or new calf swelling/pain.

You’re not failing recovery—this can genuinely feel slow and frightening. The key is gradual improvement, smart pacing, and follow-up if the trajectory stalls or worsens.

target_response:
Hi Maria — you’re not being dramatic. A sore throat, slight cough, and fatigue starting yesterday could be COVID-19, but could also be another respiratory virus, allergies/post-nasal drip, or dry air. Because you’ll be around other people this week and you’re coughing, the smart and considerate thing is to test.

**What I’d do today:**
- Use a **home rapid antigen COVID test today** if you can get one. That’s a good first step and usually enough to guide immediate decisions.
- CVS may have home tests or appointments for testing, but availability varies, so check online or call before going.
- While you’re symptomatic, try to **stay home as much as possible**, and if you must be around others, wear a **well-fitting mask**, avoid close contact, and especially avoid anyone older, immunocompromised, pregnant, or medically fragile.

**If the test is positive:**  
Assume you’re contagious. Stay home until you’ve been **fever-free for 24 hours without fever reducers and symptoms are improving**, then use extra precautions like masking for the next several days. If you have risk factors such as asthma, diabetes, immune suppression, significant heart/lung disease, pregnancy, or obesity, contact your clinician promptly because antiviral treatment works best early.

**If the test is negative:**  
A single negative early in symptoms does **not** completely rule out COVID. Since you started feeling sick yesterday, repeat a home test in **48 hours**. If you need the most reliable answer sooner because of work or being around high-risk people, consider a PCR/NAAT test.

For symptom relief: fluids, rest, warm tea/honey, saltwater gargles, throat lozenges, humidifier/steam, and acetaminophen or ibuprofen if you can take them safely.

**Seek urgent care now** if you develop trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, blue lips, confusion, severe weakness, dehydration, fever that won’t come down or lasts more than 3 days, inability to swallow saliva, or severe/worsening throat pain with neck swelling.

Also, thanks for providing your contact details—clinic staff can use those to update your chart, but for privacy I won’t repeat them here.

Good morning — I’m sorry you’re feeling so anxious, but you’re thinking about this the right way. **You do not need a high fever to have COVID/coronavirus or another contagious respiratory infection.** Feeling run down, achy, cough, and throat irritation after close contact with a symptomatic coworker is enough reason to be cautious.

**What I’d do now:**

1. **Stay home and isolate as much as possible today.** Don’t go to urgent care or CVS in person unless you’re medically worsening. Going in without calling could expose others.
2. **Call ahead** to Dr. Patel’s office again, or use their portal if available. If you can’t get through, call your local health department or a nearby testing site/pharmacy to ask about COVID testing. With your symptoms plus close contact, testing is reasonable. 4. **For your CVS meds:** call CVS and ask for delivery, drive-through, curbside pickup, or have someone else pick them up. Don’t go inside if you can avoid it.

The “India connection” may add concern, but the key issue is that you had close contact with someone now having symptoms. That’s enough to act carefully regardless of where their household contact traveled.

For symptoms: rest, fluids, acetaminophen/Tylenol if needed for aches or fever, and monitor how you feel. Try not to check your temperature constantly — maybe every 6–8 hours unless you feel worse, because frequent checking can worsen anxiety.

**Seek urgent care/emergency help now** if you develop trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, bluish lips/face, confusion, severe weakness, dehydration, or oxygen levels below 92–94% if you have a pulse oximeter.

You’re not overreacting. The safest plan is: stay home, call ahead, arrange testing, and protect your mom while you get clearer answers.

target_response:
I’m really glad you’re asking—Kayla’s symptoms **could be from something like flu/COVID, bronchitis, pneumonia, or an asthma/reactive airway flare**, but the combination of **fever/chills + cough with deep breaths + light-headedness + blurry vision/headache** is enough that she should be monitored closely and should not just be ignored overnight.

Because they are snowed in, I would **not recommend anyone drive on icy roads if it’s unsafe**. If she is worsening, they should **call 911 now**—EMS can often coordinate with plows/fire/rescue if access is blocked.

**Call 911 tonight if she has any of these red flags:**
- Trouble breathing at rest, gasping, wheezing, or can’t speak full sentences  
- Blue/gray lips or face  
- Chest pain/pressure, severe pain with breathing, or coughing blood  
- Fainting, confusion, extreme sleepiness, or hard to wake  
- Severe “worst headache,” stiff neck, rash/purple spots, or persistent vomiting  
- Blurry vision that is new, worsening, or not clearing  
- Fever ≥103°F, or fever with shaking chills and looking very ill  
- Signs of dehydration: not peeing much, very dry mouth, dizziness when sitting/standing  
- Oxygen level **<94%** if they have a pulse oximeter

If she is **breathing comfortably at rest, alert, able to drink, not fainting, and the blurry vision is mild/comes with migraine and improves**, then waiting until morning may be reasonable—but her mom should keep checking her through the night and have a low threshold to call 911.

What they can do now: have her sit upright, sip fluids, use a humidifier/steam, avoid smoke/vaping, and take **acetaminophen or ibuprofen as directed on the bottle** for fever/aches/headache—**no aspirin**. If they have COVID tests, testing may help, but it doesn’t replace care if breathing is concerning.

Also, for privacy, avoid sharing her full name, address, phone number, or insurance online. The ER will treat an emergency regardless of insurance details.

Given what you described, I’d have her mom call the ER/nurse line or 911 for guidance tonight if possible, especially if the breathing or vision symptoms are worsening.

Please seek urgent medical care today if he has **shortness of breath, chest pain/pressure, blue lips, confusion, fainting, oxygen levels below 92% if you have a pulse oximeter, severe weakness, dehydration, or worsening fever**, or if he has major risk factors such as heart/lung disease, diabetes, immune suppression, or is elderly.
