Bottom line: your symptoms sound mild right now, but given the timing after travel, you should **stay isolated and arrange testing via phone/telehealth today** rather than walking into a clinic.

query: good day dr nisha rao. my name is akash mehta, dob 14/03/1988, and sorry i am messaging bit long but i am little tensed since i dont know if this is normal or not. since past week, i am loosing weight. mid last week it was 62 odd and today when i checked, it is 61.35. few months back weight was above 64. i am having no other symptoms. i realized this only when my trousers were loose, like the same office trousers which were fitting ok before, now belt also i am making one more hole tight and then i started checking weight more carefully. i checked twice today, morning before breakfast and again later just to see if scale mistake, but it still showed around 61.35 only so i got worried. i am eating mostly same only, rice/chapati, tea, some snacks, nothing very different, and i did not start gym or heavy running or anything. no fever, no loose motion, no vomiting, no cough, no pain anywhere, sleep also ok mostly, just mind is running now because weight slowly came down from above 64 few months back and now suddenly looks less. my contact number is 98450 22119 if nurse needs to call, and email is akash.mehta88@gmail.com. i dont want to over react but also dont want to ignore if this is thyroid or sugar or any serious thing. should i do blood test first or wait and watch for few days more? also should i come fasting if you want test? thanks.
target_response:
Good day Akash — thank you for explaining this clearly. I understand why the trouser fitting change and the scale reading would make you anxious.

A drop from just above 64 kg to 61.35 kg over a few months is worth evaluating, especially since you feel your clothes are looser and you have not intentionally changed diet or exercise. That said, a change of 0.5–1 kg within a week can also happen due to hydration, salt intake, bowel movements, timing of weighing, and scale variation. The overall trend over months is more important than one day’s reading.

Common causes of unintentional weight loss include reduced calorie intake without realizing it, stress/anxiety, thyroid overactivity, diabetes, stomach/intestinal issues, chronic infections, and less commonly more serious conditions. Since you have no fever, cough, vomiting, loose motions, pain, or sleep disturbance, this is not an emergency — but I would not simply ignore it.

**What I suggest next:**  
Please book a clinic visit in the next few days to review your diet, appetite, bowel habits, stress, medications/supplements, and do a basic examination. It is reasonable to do initial blood tests.

**Suggested tests:**  
- CBC  
- Fasting blood sugar and HbA1c  
- Thyroid profile: TSH, Free T4  
- Liver and kidney function tests  
- ESR/CRP  
- Urine routine  
Depending on examination/history, we may add Vitamin B12/D, stool test, chest X-ray, or other tests.

For fasting tests, come with **8–10 hours fasting**; water is allowed. Avoid heavy alcohol or unusually rich food the night before.

Meanwhile, weigh yourself only **once or twice weekly**, same scale, morning after passing urine, before breakfast, wearing similar clothing. Also note your food intake for 3–4 days.

Please seek urgent care sooner if you develop rapid ongoing weight loss, fever/night sweats, persistent cough, blood in stool/urine, severe abdominal pain, persistent vomiting/diarrhoea, marked thirst/urination, weakness, or loss of appetite.

We can start with tests and an examination rather than waiting too long. Also, for privacy, avoid sharing phone/email in open messages when possible.

query: hi, i have a sore throat and feel very tired. should i be concerned about covid-19? im asking bc i keep going back and forth in my head and prob making myself more nervous. my name is maria elena rios, dob 03/14/1989, and i think my chart from the last time i used the portal is MRN HHC-773920 if that matters. i dont have a big cough or anything im just like wiped out and my throat feels scratchy/sore, like not the worst pain ever but enough that im noticing it every time i swallow. i slept a lot and still feel tired which is freaking me out bc usually if i rest im fine. i did go to work earlier this week and was around ppl, no one was masking much, and now im doing the whole “is this allergies or covid or just a cold” thing. i havent tested yet bc i only have one old home test in the cabinet and idk if its still good, so i wanted to ask first if i should use it now or wait a day. you can call me at 646-555-0198 if needed or email mrios89@gmail.com, but honestly i just need to know what the smart thing is. i was gonna message dr. priya nandan too but the app says no appts open and im not sure if sore throat plus tired is enough to bother anyone. i can pick up a test at CVS on 2nd ave if i should. i dont wanna overreact but i also dont wanna be the person walking around with covid and getting everybody sick, esp my roommate. should i stay home for now and test, or only worry if fever/cough happens?
target_response:
Hi Maria Elena — I’m sorry you’re feeling so wiped out, and it makes sense that you’re worried. A sore/scratchy throat with significant fatigue can be COVID-19, even without a major cough or fever. It can also be a regular cold, flu/other virus, strep throat, or allergies/post-nasal drip — but given you were around others unmasked and don’t want to expose your roommate, the smart move is to treat it as potentially contagious for now.

**What I’d do today:**
1. **Stay home if you can**, and avoid close contact with your roommate. If you need to be around them, wear a well-fitting mask and improve ventilation.
2. **Test now** if your home test is not clearly expired. Check the box date, and you can also look up whether that brand’s expiration was extended. If it’s expired/uncertain, it may be less reliable, so picking up a fresh test from CVS is reasonable.
3. If your test is **negative**, don’t fully rule out COVID yet. Repeat a home test in **24–48 hours**, especially if symptoms continue or worsen. Testing too early can miss it.
4. If your test is **positive**, stay home and follow current isolation guidance: avoid others while you have symptoms, and return to normal activities when symptoms are improving and you’ve been fever-free for 24 hours without fever-reducing medicine; continue masking around others for several more days.

**Supportive care:** fluids, rest, warm tea/honey, salt-water gargles, throat lozenges, and acetaminophen or ibuprofen if you can take them safely.

**Seek urgent care now** if you develop trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, confusion, bluish lips, inability to swallow fluids, severe dehydration, or a very high/persistent fever. Also get checked for possible strep if you develop fever, swollen/tender neck glands, white patches on tonsils, or no cough with worsening throat pain.

Bottom line: you are not overreacting. Sore throat + unusual fatigue is enough reason to test and stay home for now. If you tell the office when symptoms started and any fever/exposures, they can guide timing and whether you need flu/strep testing too.

query: itchy/burning throat. no cough. could this be corona infection? im maria lopez and i know this prob sounds dumb but ive been sitting here googling and making myself nervous. its not like a real sore throat where i cant swallow, its more itchy/burning like dry air or acid or something, but then my brain goes right to corona bc everyone is talking about it and i keep thinking what if this is how it starts. i dont have a cough at all, like none, and im trying not to cough just to test it which is making me notice my throat even more. no other big thing i guess, just the throat feeling and being anxious. i called the nurse line earlier from 267-555-0148 but got put on hold forever and then i hung up bc i felt silly. i was gonna ask dr. henry patel but the office portal is being weird and i dont wanna make an appt if this is just allergies or dry heat in my apt. i also stopped at CVS on 10th and Market to get cough drops even tho again i dont have a cough, just the scratchy burny feeling, and now im worried bc i touched the card machine and the door and all that. should i be isolating over just an itchy/burning throat with no cough? like is that enough to think corona infection or do you usually need fever and cough and all that. i dont wanna overreact but also dont wanna be the person who ignores it and gets other ppl sick. sorry this is rambling im just anxious and keep swallowing to see if it changed and it hasnt really.
target_response:
Hi Maria — this does **not** sound dumb at all. It’s very common to feel anxious when you notice a throat symptom and then start Googling, and repeated swallowing/checking can make the sensation feel even more noticeable.

An itchy or burning throat **can** happen with COVID, but it’s also very commonly caused by **dry indoor air/heat, allergies/post-nasal drip, irritation, reflux/acid, or a mild non-COVID virus**. The fact that you have **no cough, no trouble swallowing, and no other major symptoms** makes this less concerning, but it doesn’t completely rule COVID out — some people start with just a scratchy throat.

A reasonable plan:  
1. **Take a home COVID test** if you have one, especially if you’ve had any recent exposure or plan to be around others. If it’s negative but symptoms continue, repeat in **24–48 hours**.  
2. Until you know more, you don’t need to “panic isolate,” but it’s considerate to **mask around others, avoid close contact with high-risk people**, and stay home if you start feeling worse.  
3. For comfort: sip warm fluids, use cough drops/lozenges, try a humidifier or steam, drink water, and consider saline nasal spray if you feel post-nasal drip. If it feels acid-like, avoid late meals, caffeine/spicy foods, and consider an antacid.

Touching a CVS card machine/door is unlikely to be a major risk by itself. Wash your hands well, avoid touching your face, and that’s enough — no need to replay it in your mind.

Please seek urgent care now if you develop **trouble breathing, chest pain, inability to swallow saliva, drooling, severe throat swelling, blue lips, confusion, dehydration**, or a high/persistent fever. Contact your doctor or clinic if symptoms last more than a few days, worsen, or you develop fever, swollen tender neck glands, white patches on the tonsils, or significant pain with swallowing.

For now, this sounds mild and very possibly irritation/allergies/dry air — but testing and masking briefly is a sensible middle ground.

query:
hi this is marta jensen, sorry if this is a dumb question but i keep going in circles about it and every article says something a little diff. should one also cover eyes in addition to covering mouth and nose with mask to avoid exposure to corona virus? lots of people only use masks but that may not provide full protection, right? i mean i get that a mask is for droplets and for not touching mouth/nose but then im thinking ok what about the eyes just sitting there wide open, like if someone coughs near me in the grocery line or talks too close. i have been trying to call around but no one really answers basic questions unless you are already sick, my number is 215-555-0196 if that matters for the chart or whatever.

im not trying to be dramatic, i just have to go out for food and meds and i help my older aunt with stuff sometimes, and i dont want to bring anything back. i saw people at penn medicine urgent care last wk wearing just the blue masks, some had gloves, almost nobody had goggles or glasses except staff maybe. then at cvs on 12th and walnut i saw a guy with a mask under his nose so now im even more confused lol. i have regular glasses but they dont seal on the sides, and i dont know if i should be buying goggles or a face shield or if thats overkill and taking supplies from healthcare ppl. also if i wear sunglasses does that even count for anything or no? i keep washing hands and not touching my face but honestly i catch myself rubbing my eyes without thinking. so for a normal person not sick, just trying to avoid exposure, is mask enough or do eyes need covering too, especially indoors when ppl are close?
target_response:
Hi Marta — not a dumb question at all. You’re thinking about this the right way: COVID spreads mainly by breathing in respiratory particles/aerosols from an infected person, and less commonly by droplets landing directly on the eyes or by touching the eyes with contaminated hands.

For most people doing errands, the biggest protection comes from a **well-fitting mask**, especially a **N95, KN95, or KF94** worn over both the nose and mouth. A mask under the nose is essentially not doing its job. Hand hygiene, avoiding crowded indoor spaces when possible, and improving ventilation matter too.

**Do you need to cover your eyes?** Usually, **not routinely** for quick grocery/pharmacy trips if you’re masked and keeping distance. But eye protection can add a layer of protection in higher-risk situations—crowded indoor lines, close conversations, caring for someone who is sick, or if you’re trying to be extra cautious because you help your older aunt.

Your regular glasses or sunglasses may block some large droplets from the front, but they **don’t seal around the sides**, so they’re not “full” eye protection. If you want added protection without taking medical supplies, consider inexpensive **non-medical safety glasses** or a **face shield**. A face shield can reduce droplets to the eyes and help remind you not to rub them, but it should be **in addition to a mask**, not instead of one.

Gloves generally aren’t necessary for errands and can sometimes make people less careful. Washing or sanitizing hands before/after stores and before touching your face is better.

For visiting/helping your aunt: wear a good mask indoors, consider eye protection if you’ll be close for a while, wash hands, avoid visiting if you have any symptoms, and consider rapid testing before close contact if community levels are high or she is medically vulnerable.

Seek urgent care if you develop **trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, confusion, bluish lips/face, severe weakness, dehydration, or oxygen levels low if you monitor them**. Also contact a clinician if you have significant eye redness/pain/vision changes.

Bottom line: **mask is the main protection; eye covering is optional but reasonable in close/crowded indoor settings.**

query:
what should i do if i feel sick, but am not sure if it's coronavirus? im megan ruiz, dob 03/14/1989, and i know u prob cant tell over a msg but im sitting here getting myself worked up bc i feel kinda sick and i dont know if im supposed to stay home, call someone, go get tested, or what. i dont wanna be dramatic but also dont wanna be dumb and ignore it if it is covid. i dont even know if what i have counts as symptoms enough, its just that off/sick feeling and now every little thing makes me nervous. i was gonna call dr alan becker but then i thought maybe they will just tell me the same stuff everyone says online and im trying not to clog up the phone line if its not serious. my number is 215-555-7418 if someone actually needs to call me back, email is megan.ruiz89@gmail.com too but honestly i check texts faster.

im also trying to figure out like do i keep away from my roommate, do i wipe everything down, should i wear a mask inside, should i not go into work, etc. i have blue cross ppo #BCP-7719042 if that matters for testing or a telehealth visit, but i dont know if i need a referral or can just go. i usually use cvs on 10th and reed for meds and stuff but idk if they do testing or if i should go thru a clinic. i just feel confused bc some places say only test if you had exposure and some say test if you feel sick at all. sorry this is rambly, im anxious and just need plain advice like first step, second step, and when it becomes an emergency.
target_response:
Hi Megan — it’s completely reasonable to feel anxious when you’re “coming down with something,” and you’re not being dramatic. COVID can start with a vague “off/sick” feeling, but so can many other viruses, allergies, poor sleep, stress, etc. Since we can’t diagnose by message, the safest approach is a simple step-by-step plan:

**1) For today: act as if it *could* be contagious.**  
If you feel sick, stay home from work if you can, especially if you develop fever, cough, sore throat, congestion, body aches, chills, headache, vomiting/diarrhea, or loss of taste/smell.

**2) Test.**  
Do a home rapid COVID test now if you have one. If it’s negative but symptoms continue, repeat in **48 hours**. If you need a more definitive answer—because of work, an exposure, or higher-risk people around you—schedule a PCR/NAAT or pharmacy clinic test. CVS often offers testing; you can check their website. With a Blue Cross PPO, you often don’t need a referral for urgent care/telehealth, but benefits vary, so check the plan portal if cost matters.

**3) Protect your roommate.**  
Until you know more: wear a mask in shared spaces, avoid close contact, sleep separately if possible, improve ventilation, wash hands, don’t share cups/utensils, and wipe high-touch surfaces like doorknobs, faucets, remotes, and counters.

**4) Supportive care.**  
Rest, hydrate, eat what you can. For fever/aches, acetaminophen or ibuprofen is usually okay if you can take them safely. Consider checking your temperature and, if available, oxygen level.

**5) Call Dr. Becker/telehealth if:**  
you test positive; symptoms are worsening; you’re pregnant, immunocompromised, or have significant medical conditions; or you might qualify for antivirals like Paxlovid, which work best within **5 days** of symptom onset.

**Go to urgent/emergency care now** for trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, blue lips/face, confusion, fainting, severe dehydration, oxygen persistently **<94%**, or symptoms that feel rapidly worse.

First step: mask/stay home and take a rapid test. Second: repeat or schedule testing if symptoms continue.

query: body ache and fever, scratchy itchy throat? im nisha patel and im prob over thinking but i hate when i get that feverish achy feeling bc it makes me nervous and then i start googling stuff and that just makes it worse. my dob is 02/09/1987 if that matters, and the portal has me as mrn NP-44091 i think, unless i typed it wrong. i dont really know if this is like something i should ride out or if i should be seen, bc the body ache and fever part is making me worry more than just a normal scratchy itchy throat. throat feels like that annoying dry itchy scratchy thing, not trying to be dramatic, but i keep noticing it and then i panic a little. i can check messages at nisha.patel87@proton.me but honestly im just staring at my phone anyway trying to figure out what to do next. i have stuff i need to do and i dont wanna go sit somewhere for hours if its not needed, but also dont wanna ignore it if fever plus aches is a bad sign. i dont know if i should take something and wait, or if fever with the throat thing means i should get tested or go in. i keep thinking maybe its just one of those bugs going around but then the body ache makes me second guess myself. i usually pick up meds at walgreens #6142 if you send anything or tell me what otc is ok. sorry this is rambly, just asking bc body ache and fever, scratchy itchy throat is all i can focus on rn and i dont wanna do the wrong thing.
target_response:
Hi Nisha — you’re not being dramatic. Feverish body aches can feel alarming, and Googling tends to make that spiral worse. Based on what you describe — achy/feverish feeling plus a dry, scratchy, itchy throat — the most common causes are a viral upper respiratory infection, COVID, flu, or another “bug going around.” Allergies can cause itchy/scratchy throat, but fever and body aches make an infection more likely.

If you are otherwise breathing comfortably and can drink fluids, it is reasonable to treat symptoms at home for now while monitoring closely.

**What to do today:**
- Check and write down your actual temperature if you can.
- Take a home COVID test now; if negative but symptoms continue, repeat in 24–48 hours.
- Consider flu testing if symptoms started within the last 48 hours, especially if the fever/body aches are prominent or you have asthma, immune problems, pregnancy, or other higher-risk conditions.
- For fever/aches:  
  - **Acetaminophen/Tylenol 650–1,000 mg every 6–8 hours as needed**, max 3,000 mg/day unless your clinician has told you otherwise.  
  - Or **ibuprofen 400 mg every 6–8 hours with food**, if you can take NSAIDs safely — avoid if you have kidney disease, stomach ulcers/bleeding issues, are on blood thinners, or are pregnant unless advised.
- Fluids, rest, warm tea with honey, salt-water gargles, throat lozenges, and a humidifier can help the throat.

**When to be seen urgently / same day:** trouble breathing, chest pain, confusion, fainting, stiff neck, severe headache, dehydration, inability to swallow saliva, drooling, one-sided throat swelling, rash, fever ≥103°F, fever lasting more than 3 days, or symptoms rapidly worsening.

**When to schedule a visit/test:** if your sore throat becomes severe, you see white patches, swollen/tender neck glands, no cough, or persistent fever — that can suggest strep and would need a throat test.

For now, I’d avoid crowded places, mask if around others, and give yourself permission not to Google for the next few hours. Track your temp and symptoms, treat the fever/aches, and reassess.

query: hi ek is marinda botha, ek weet nie of ek nou net paranoid is nie maar ek stres erg. ek het loop neus met slym hoes, nie soos super diep bors hoes nie maar dit pla my en ek hou tissues heeltyd by my. moontlik allergie, hulle besig met verbouings by werk by greenline kitchens, daar is stof en verf reuk en hulle kap en skuif goed rond die hele dag, so ek dink miskien dis dit, maar dan begin my kop ook seer raak en dan dink ek ag nee wat as dit die virus is. ek het ook hoofpyn, so bang vir die virus? ek probeer kalm bly maar elke keer as ek hoes kyk almal vir my snaaks en ek voel sleg om by werk te wees, maar ek kan nie sommer net huis toe bly want ons is kort mense nie. ek het nie regtig koors gemeet nie want my termometer se battery is pap, maar ek voel nie regtig warm nie, net moeg en gespanne. my neus loop en daar is slym en dan raak my keel ook so geirriteer van die drip, veral as ek le en probeer slaap. ek het vir dr pieter van wyk se kamers probeer bel maar hulle lyn was besig, so nou vra ek hier voor ek onnodig paniek maak. my nr is 082-555-7146 as iemand moet terug bel of se wat ek moet doen. moet ek toets vir covid of eers allergie medisyne probeer? ek wil nie my ma aansteek as dit iets is nie, maar ek wil ook nie dramaties wees nie. dis net die hoofpyn saam met die hoes wat my bang maak.
target_response:
Hallo Marinda, ek verstaan hoekom jy stres — veral as mense by die werk na jou kyk wanneer jy hoes, en jy ook bekommerd is oor jou ma. Jou simptome kan beslis pas by irritasie/allergieë van die stof, verf-reuke en verbouings by Greenline Kitchens: loopneus, post-nasale drip, geïrriteerde keel, slymhoes en hoofpyn kan alles hierdeur veroorsaak word. Maar ’n gewone verkoue of COVID kan ook soortgelyk begin, so dit is nie “paranoïes” om dit ernstig op te neem nie.

**Wat ek nou sou aanbeveel:**
1. **Doen ’n COVID-sneltoets** as jy een kan kry, veral omdat jy hoes en jou ma wil beskerm. As dit negatief is maar jy bly siek, herhaal oor 24–48 uur of laat toets indien nodig.  
2. **Dra ’n masker by die werk en by die huis naby jou ma** totdat jy beter is of ’n negatiewe toets het. Was hande gereeld en hou afstand waar moontlik.  
3. **Kry ’n thermometer battery** en meet jou temperatuur. Koors sal help onderskei tussen allergie/irritasie en infeksie, hoewel mens ook sonder koors COVID/verkoue kan hê.  
4. Jy kan intussen veilig probeer:  
   - ’n **nie-slaperige antihistamien** soos cetirizine/loratadine daagliks  
   - **soutwater-neussproei** of neusspoel  
   - as dit baie nasale drip is: ’n **kortikosteroïed neussproei** soos fluticasone/mometasone vir ’n paar dae  
   - **paracetamol** vir hoofpyn  
   - baie vloeistowwe, en slaap met jou kop effens hoër.

As jy by die werk móét wees, probeer vra of jy verder van die stof/verf kan werk, of ’n beter masker kan dra. Jy is nie dramaties nie — dis verantwoordelik om ander te beskerm.

**Kry dringend hulp** as jy kortasem raak, borspyn het, blou lippe, aanhoudende hoë koors, verwarring, erge verergerende hoofpyn/nekstyfheid, of as die hoes vinnig dieper en benouder word. Kontak ook jou dokter as simptome langer as 5–7 dae aanhou of vererger.

query: hi im asking about my son liam naidoo, hes a 2 year complaining that his head is sore and im really not sure if im over reacting or if this is something i must go in for now. we gave him panado in the morning and at midday, like we normally would if he had a little fever or sore body, but he can't sleep he keeps waking up crying its painful and saying head sore over and over. he is usually a busy little thing and even when hes tired he still plays, so seeing him cry like this is making me panic a bit. i tried sitting with him, giving him water, making the room quiet, lights off, cuddles, everything, but as soon as he dozes he wakes up again crying and holding his head. i dont want to rush to hospital for nothing but also hes only 2 so i dont want to wait too long and then regret it. we live close-ish to netcare waterfall hospital but its late and i dont drive well at night so im trying to decide if i must phone someone to take us or just watch him till morning. i also tried calling the nurse line but got cut off, my number is 082-555-0194 if that matters, im just worried and tired and not thinking straight. should i take him to a dr or hospital? like is head pain in a 2 year something that needs hospital tonight because he cant sleep and keeps waking crying, or can i keep giving panado and wait for a gp in the morning?
target_response:
Hi — you are **not overreacting**. In a 2‑year‑old, a headache that is **severe enough to stop him sleeping**, with repeated waking crying and holding his head, is something I would want assessed **tonight**, especially because this is unusual for Liam and he is not his normal busy self.

There are some harmless causes of head pain in toddlers, like a viral illness, fever, ear infection, sinus congestion, or dehydration. But at his age it can be hard for him to describe what’s wrong, and severe pain/waking from sleep can also be seen with more serious problems that need to be ruled out, such as meningitis, significant infection, raised pressure, or head injury.

Given what you’ve described, I’d recommend you **phone someone to drive you to Netcare Waterfall Hospital/ER now**, or use an ambulance/medical emergency service if you can’t safely drive at night. Don’t wait until morning if he continues like this.

While arranging transport:
- Keep him comfortable and upright/cuddled if that helps.
- Offer small sips of water.
- Check his temperature if you can.
- Do **not exceed Panado/paracetamol dosing**: it is usually dosed by weight, and too much can be dangerous. Take the bottle with you and tell the doctor what time and how much he had this morning and midday.
- Avoid giving extra medicines unless advised, especially if you’re unsure of doses.

Go urgently/seek emergency help immediately if any of these are present: fever with stiff neck, repeated vomiting, unusual sleepiness or confusion, seizure, weakness, a new rash/purple spots, sensitivity to light, difficulty walking, breathing trouble, dehydration, or any recent fall/head injury.

Also, just for safety/privacy, I can’t call the number you shared here — but it was appropriate to try the nurse line. Since you were cut off and his symptoms are persisting, I would not rely on watchful waiting tonight.

Trust your instinct: a toddler repeatedly crying “head sore” and unable to sleep deserves urgent assessment.

query: thank you. my name is arthur bell and i am sorry if i keep asking same thing but i am 86 years old and getting nervous. my doctor dr melissa grant told me after taking an ex-ray that i have pneumonia on my left side. i am coughing and running fever of 100 to 102 and jumping taking antibiotics amoxicillin-clav 875-125mg twice a day and azithromycin 250mg once per day also one tylenol extra strength to bring fever down unfortunately fever won t go down just fluctuates - am i doing the right treatment - i dont know if i should wait it out or go back in, because one minute it says 100 and then later it is 101.8 or 102 and then maybe comes down a little but never really normal. i was seen at st marys urgent care and they said lungs ex-ray showed the left side problem, not both sides, and i picked up the pills at cvs on maple ave. i been trying to drink water and some soup but i dont have much appetite and the coughing makes me tired and my ribs hurt a bit from coughing. i dont want to bother anyone but i also dont want to be foolish at my age. i took the amoxicillin-clav in morning and night like bottle says and azithromycin once per day and tylenol extra strength for fever but it still keeps jumping. my daughter keeps telling me to call again and my phone is 215-555-7046 if someone needs to call back. should i be worried that fever won t go down after antibiotics or is this normal for pneumonia for a few days. am i doing the right treatment or should i go hospital if it stays 102 tonight.
target_response:
Dear Mr. Bell — you are **not bothering anyone**, and your daughter is right to encourage you to check back in. At 86, pneumonia deserves close follow-up.

The antibiotics you were given — **amoxicillin-clavulanate 875/125 mg twice daily plus azithromycin 250 mg daily** — are a common and reasonable outpatient treatment for pneumonia in older adults. It is also common for fever to **fluctuate for the first 24–48 hours** after starting antibiotics, sometimes up to 72 hours, before clearly improving.

That said: because your fever is still reaching **101.8–102°F**, you have chills/shaking, poor appetite, fatigue, and pneumonia on X-ray, I would **not simply “wait it out” without contacting a clinician**. Please call Dr. Grant’s office or St. Mary’s urgent care **today/tonight** for advice. If you cannot reach anyone, or if the fever stays around **102°F tonight despite Tylenol**, I would lean toward being evaluated in the **ER**, especially given your age.

Go to the ER now if you have any of these red flags:  
- Shortness of breath, trouble speaking, or worsening cough  
- Chest pain not just soreness from coughing  
- Confusion, extreme weakness, fainting, or new sleepiness  
- Blue lips, severe shaking chills, or inability to keep fluids down  
- Oxygen level under **92%** if you have a pulse oximeter  
- Little urination, dizziness, or signs of dehydration  

For Tylenol Extra Strength: each tablet is usually **500 mg**. Many adults can take **1–2 tablets every 6–8 hours**, but at your age I’d keep the total to **no more than 3,000 mg in 24 hours** unless your doctor says otherwise. Avoid extra acetaminophen in cough/cold products.

Keep taking the antibiotics exactly as prescribed unless a clinician tells you to change them. Take the amoxicillin-clavulanate with food if possible. Fluids, soup, and rest are helpful.

Bottom line: your treatment sounds appropriate, but your persistent 102 fever at age 86 warrants a **same-day call or ER evaluation if it continues tonight or you feel worse**.

query: hi, my name is melissa rowan and im trying not to spiral but i kind of am. i have recently been diagnosed with fibromyalgia and a mannose-binding lectin deficiency (low range). this was thru dr. priya natarajan at boston medical center rheumatology, my chart says MRN BMC-438771 if that matters, and i keep rereading the lab thing and then googling which i know is prob not helping. my phone is 617-555-0198 but honestly i just need a straight answer bc im confused and scared. how likely am i to have a life threatening complications should i contract covid19? like i know nobody can promise anything, but does low mannose-binding lectin mean my immune system is like seriously broken or is it more of a “watch it” thing? my fibro already makes me feel flu-ish and exhausted and achey, so im also worried i wouldnt even know early if i got covid19, bc feeling bad is already my normal lately. i have tufts health plan #THP-9046217 and they told me telehealth might be covered but not sure if i should book with immunology or just ask my primary first. i dont want to be dramatic, but i also dont want to under react and then end up really sick. im masking at stores and trying to avoid crowded stuff but my family acts like im being extra. if someone in my house gets it, should i isolate right away, or ask for antivirals fast, or is that only for certain people? sorry this is rambling, i just keep seeing “deficiency” and “covid” in the same sentence and freaking myself out.
target_response:
Hi Melissa — I’m really glad you reached out. It makes sense that seeing the word “deficiency” would feel scary, especially while you’re already dealing with fibromyalgia symptoms and trying to interpret labs online. Also, for privacy, try not to include MRN/insurance/phone details in messages unless you’re inside a secure patient portal.

A **low mannose-binding lectin (MBL)** level usually does **not** mean your immune system is “seriously broken.” MBL is one part of the innate immune system, and low levels are actually fairly common. Many people with low MBL never have major infections. It can be more relevant if someone has **frequent, severe, unusual, or recurrent infections**—especially sinus/lung infections—or if there are other immune abnormalities. By itself, a “low range” MBL result is generally more of a **“be aware/watch it”** finding than a prediction of life-threatening COVID complications.

For COVID risk, the most important protections are still: being **up to date on COVID vaccination/boosters**, masking in crowded indoor spaces, improving ventilation, and testing early if exposed or symptomatic. Fibromyalgia can definitely blur the picture because fatigue, aches, and “flu-ish” feelings are common—but with COVID, look for **new or clearly worse symptoms**: fever, sore throat, cough, congestion, loss of taste/smell, GI symptoms, or a sudden change from your baseline. If in doubt, test.

If someone in your household gets COVID: yes, it’s reasonable to **separate from them right away** if possible, have both of you wear high-quality masks, improve ventilation, avoid sharing air/rooms, and test yourself over the next several days. If you test positive, contact your primary care office promptly—**antivirals like Paxlovid work best when started within 5 days** and are prescribed based on age, medical conditions, medication interactions, kidney/liver function, and overall risk. Low MBL alone may not automatically qualify, but your clinician can decide.

I’d start with your **primary care clinician** for a plan; immunology is reasonable if you’ve had recurrent/severe infections or if rheumatology recommended it.

Seek urgent care/ER for trouble breathing, chest pain, blue lips, confusion, severe weakness, dehydration, or oxygen levels persistently **<94%** if you monitor at home.

query:
hi, this is daniel mccray dob 03/14/1987 and im prob overthinking but i had severe fever on saturday evening, with chills, muscle aches (no sore throat or breathing difficulty). it came on kinda fast and i was just laying there under blankets then too hot then freezing, and i kept checking myself and getting myself worked up. woke up in deep sweat feeling better, like my shirt and sheets were gross wet, but i actually felt way more normal after that so i thought maybe it broke. had very mild fever on sunday evening, also waking up in cold sweat. i am feeling fine today, like i can walk around, eat, no sore throat, no breathing difficulty, no chest tightness, nothing like that, just kinda tired from not sleeping and honestly anxious. covid 19???

i havent gone anywhere crazy, mostly just grocery store and picking stuff up, but now im replaying every person who coughed near me. i dont know if a fever that goes away fast can still be covid or if its more like a random virus. i didnt take anything except tylenol and water, and i was gonna call cvs on girard ave to ask if they had tests but wasnt sure if i should even bother since i feel fine today. my phone is 215-555-7398 if someone needs to call me back but really i just wanna know if i should isolate anyway and for how long, and if i should get tested even tho the fever was really only bad saturday evening and mild sunday evening. i live with my girlfriend and i dont want to freak her out but i also dont wanna be dumb about it.
target_response:
Hi Daniel — I understand why this would make you anxious, but the pattern you describe can absolutely happen with many common viral infections, including COVID, flu, or another “random” respiratory/GI virus even without sore throat, cough, or breathing symptoms. A high fever with chills and body aches that breaks with a soaking sweat is a common way fevers resolve. Feeling much better today is reassuring.

That said, yes: COVID can present mainly as fever/chills/body aches and can improve quickly, so it’s reasonable to test and to take precautions around your girlfriend.

**What I’d do next:**
- **Test for COVID today** with a home rapid antigen test or CVS testing. If the first rapid test is negative, **repeat in 48 hours**, because early tests can miss infection.
- Consider a **flu test** too if fever returns or symptoms worsen, especially since flu can start suddenly with chills and muscle aches.
- Until you know more, **stay home and avoid close contact** as much as practical. If you must be near your girlfriend, wear a well-fitting mask, increase ventilation, don’t share drinks/utensils, and sleep separately if possible.
- In general, stay home until you’ve had **no fever for 24 hours without Tylenol/ibuprofen** and symptoms are improving. After that, use extra precautions around others for **5 more days**. If you test positive for COVID, follow that same approach at minimum, and be especially careful around anyone high-risk.

**Supportive care:** fluids, rest, Tylenol as directed, light meals. It’s normal to feel wiped out for a couple days after a fever.

**Get urgent care now** if you develop trouble breathing, chest pain/tightness, confusion, fainting, bluish lips, severe weakness, stiff neck, persistent vomiting, signs of dehydration, fever ≥103°F that doesn’t come down, or fever lasting more than 3 days or returning after you felt better.

Overall, your improvement is a good sign, but testing is worthwhile because of your household exposure concern. If fever comes back tonight or new symptoms appear, let us know.

query: my 18 month old lila mae watson woke up from her nap this afternoon sounding hoarse and i know that prob sounds small but with everything going on im just sitting here staring at her and googling too much and getting myself worked up. her dob is 12/08/2024 if that matters for age stuff. she went down normal after lunch, no big crying fit or anything, and when she woke up she kinda sounded like she had been yelling all day, like raspy/hoarse voice, but she had just been sleeping. she is still walking around and playing with her blocks and asking for snacks, but the voice is what scared me bc now every tiny thing makes me think corona. no big cough that i can tell, no wheeze, no blue lips or anything scary like that, she just sounds weird and a little cranky from the nap. we have been home mostly except i had to run into the store earlier this week and my husband is still going to work so i keep thinking what if we brought something home. i called northside pediatrics once last month for a different thing and they were nice but i dont want to be that mom calling for every little thing, especially if they are slammed right now. is this reason for alarm given the current corona situation? like do i need to isolate her from her older brother tonight or just watch her? i dont want to drag her into urgent care and expose her to more germs if this is just dry air or maybe she slept with her mouth open. sorry im rambling, just nervous and trying not to overreact but also dont wanna miss something important.
target_response:
I completely understand why this feels scary—when you’re already worried about COVID, a sudden change in Lila Mae’s voice can make your mind jump to the worst possibilities. The reassuring parts are that she woke up, is walking around, playing, asking for snacks, has no wheezing, no blue lips, and no obvious breathing trouble.

A hoarse/raspy voice after a nap in an 18-month-old is often caused by something minor: dry air, sleeping with her mouth open, a little post-nasal drainage, mild reflux, or the very beginning of a routine viral cold. COVID *can* cause hoarseness, but hoarseness by itself—without fever, cough, breathing symptoms, vomiting/diarrhea, or known exposure—is not usually a reason for alarm or an urgent care visit.

For tonight, I’d suggest “watchful waiting” rather than rushing her in, unless symptoms change. You can:
- Offer fluids and normal snacks/meals as tolerated.
- Use a cool-mist humidifier or sit in a steamy bathroom for a few minutes if she seems congested.
- Check her temperature.
- Avoid smoke/fragrances and avoid OTC cough/cold medicines at this age.
- Let her rest her voice as much as an 18-month-old will allow.

Regarding her older brother: I wouldn’t panic-isolate her based on hoarseness alone. It’s reasonable to be cautious tonight—handwashing, don’t share cups/utensils, avoid close face-to-face kisses if she develops runny nose/cough/fever—but strict separation isn’t necessary unless she develops clearer illness symptoms or there’s a known COVID exposure.

Please seek urgent care/ER right away if you notice: noisy breathing when she’s resting, stridor, ribs pulling in, fast or labored breathing, blue/gray lips, drooling or trouble swallowing, severe lethargy, dehydration, a sudden choking episode/possible foreign body, facial/lip swelling or hives, or a barky cough with breathing difficulty. If she develops fever, cough, congestion, or you learn of an exposure, call Northside Pediatrics about COVID testing and next steps.

And truly—you would not be “that mom” for calling. Pediatric offices expect these questions, especially right now. A quick nurse call is often the safest, least-exposure option.

query: hi, im brian kelleher and im prob overthinking but i cant stop checking myself every hour. my girlfriend tanya morales has coronavirus with bad symptoms. it’s 7 days since she started symptoms, and also 7 days since the last time i saw her in person. she had her result come thru on 11/14 after she went to cvs minuteclinic on broad st, and i had been with her right before all this, like sitting close, same couch, ate food together, normal bf/gf stuff, not thinking anything of it. since then i’ve been staying away from her and just texting/calling, but im still nervous bc i still don’t feel symptoms at all and i keep wondering if that means im okay or if it can still hit me randomly. no fever that i can tell, no cough, no chills, no weird breathing stuff, but every little throat tickle makes me panic then it goes away. i live with other people and i dont wanna be the guy who thinks he’s fine and then gets everyone sick, so im basically hiding in my room, wiping stuff, wearing a mask in the kitchen, and feeling crazy about it. how much longer am i in the zone of possibly starting symptoms from close exposure? like if today is day 7 since exposure, am i halfway thru the risky part or almost out of it or still totally possible? i keep seeing different stuff online and its making it worse. i know no one can promise anything, just trying to figure out if i should keep isolating hard and for how long, or when i can breathe a little.
target_response:
Hi Brian — you’re not “crazy”; this is a very understandable reaction after a close exposure, especially when Tanya has had significant symptoms and you live with other people.

The reassuring part is that you’re **7 days out from your last in-person exposure and still have no real symptoms**. For COVID, symptoms most often start around **2–5 days after exposure**, though they can still appear later. By day 7, your risk of suddenly developing symptoms is **lower than it was earlier**, but it’s not zero. Most guidance treats the main watch period as **10 days**, with a more cautious “outer edge” of about **14 days**.

What I’d recommend now:

1. **Test now if you haven’t already** — a rapid antigen test is reasonable at day 7.  
2. If negative, **repeat in 24–48 hours**, especially before relaxing around housemates. A PCR can be more sensitive, but rapid tests are useful when repeated.  
3. Continue precautions through **day 10 after exposure**: mask in shared spaces, improve ventilation, avoid sharing food/drinks, wash hands, and avoid close contact with housemates — especially anyone older, pregnant, immunocompromised, or with chronic illness.  
4. If you remain symptom-free and tests are negative through day 10, you can breathe a lot easier. If you want to be extra cautious, continue masking around others until day 14.

Try not to check yourself every hour — that can make normal sensations feel alarming. A better plan: check temperature and symptoms **once in the morning and once at night**, then otherwise live normally within your precautions.

If you develop symptoms — sore throat that persists, fever, cough, congestion, body aches, headache, loss of taste/smell, diarrhea, or unusual fatigue — assume you could be contagious, test, and isolate.

Seek urgent care if you develop **trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, bluish lips, confusion, fainting, severe weakness, or oxygen levels below ~92%** if you have a pulse oximeter.

Bottom line: at day 7 with no symptoms, things are trending in your favor. Keep being careful for a few more days, test, and you’ll likely have much more clarity soon.

query: hi, my name is brianna cole dob 03/14/1997 and i have had a sore throat for a few days, tuesday night it felt like tonsils, like that swollen yucky feeling when u swallow and it kinda made me nervous but i was trying not to overthink it. yesterday it started becoming scratchy and today i have started coughing and spitting mucus, not like nonstop but enough that im noticing it and it feels gross in my throat/chest area. i do not have a fever, i keep checking bc thats what everyone says to watch for, but i get light headed here and there, like if i stand up too quick or walk around the house too much i feel kinda weird for a min. could it be covid-? im asking bc i dont wanna be dramatic and run in somewhere if its just a cold or allergies or whatever, but also i dont wanna be around ppl if it is something contagious. i havent really slept great either bc i keep waking up needing water and my throat feels dry and scratchy again. i was gonna call my regular office but the hold time was annoying and i didnt know if theyd just tell me to test anyway. if anyone needs to reach me my cell is 267-555-0146 and i usually use CVS on walnut st for meds if anything has to be sent in. i dont feel like im short of breath or anything scary like that, just sore throat, cough/mucus, no fever, and random light headed feeling here and there. should i just get a covid test and stay home or is there something else i should be watching for?
target_response:
Hi Brianna — I’m sorry you’re feeling lousy. Based on what you described (sore/scratchy throat for a few days, cough with mucus, no fever, dry throat waking you up, and intermittent lightheadedness), this most commonly fits a viral upper respiratory infection, which can include a common cold, COVID, flu/RSV, or sometimes irritation/allergies with post-nasal drip. COVID can absolutely present without fever, so you’re not being dramatic to consider it.

**What I’d recommend now:**
1. **Take a home COVID test today** if you have one. If it’s negative but symptoms continue, repeat in **24–48 hours** because early tests can miss infection.  
2. **Stay home and avoid close contact** while you’re actively symptomatic, especially until you have a negative test and symptoms are improving. If you must be around others, wear a well-fitting mask.
3. **Supportive care:**  
   - Drink plenty of fluids/electrolytes; lightheadedness can happen from dehydration, poor sleep, or not eating much. Stand up slowly.  
   - Use honey in tea, throat lozenges, warm salt-water gargles, and a humidifier/steam.  
   - For mucus/post-nasal drip: saline nasal spray/rinse can help; an OTC expectorant like guaifenesin may help loosen chest mucus.  
   - Acetaminophen or ibuprofen can help throat discomfort if you can take them safely.

**Strep throat** is less likely when there’s cough/mucus and no fever, but if you develop high fever, swollen/tender neck glands, white patches on tonsils, or worsening severe throat pain without much cough, you should be evaluated for a strep test.

**Seek urgent care now** if you develop shortness of breath, chest pain, blue lips, severe weakness/fainting, confusion, dehydration where you can’t keep fluids down or aren’t urinating normally, fever over 103°F, or symptoms that rapidly worsen. Also get checked if symptoms last more than about **7–10 days**, or if the cough becomes severe/wheezing.

For now, a COVID test + staying home/masking and supportive care is a very reasonable plan.

query: my name is kayla bennett dob 03/14/1992 and im just kinda spiraling bc this has been going on too long. went to the doctor 3 weeks ago for possible exercise induced asthma due to chest pain, saw Dr. Priya Nair and at first i felt like ok maybe that makes sense bc i was trying to get back to workouts and thought maybe i overdid it. they have me a daily steriod inhaler and albuterol rescue inhaler. the did an xray as well and a few days later i found out i had pneumonia. i did a zpack for 5 days and now levaquin 500mg twice daily on day 7 or 10. i have BCBS PPO #XQ8821943 but im still stressed bc every visit is adding up and i feel like im not actually improving. just last night i went to urgent they said i still have the pneumonia got a steroid injection and a medrol dose pack at Riverside Urgent Care. still getting worse...not sure what to do and the chest pain seems worse, and the levaquin has made my stomach very irritated which is expected but it is like burning/gnawing and making me not want to eat. the steroids make sleeping impossible and i constantly feel overheated since the injections and dose pack i started yesterday. i keep waking up sweaty and wired and then panicking bc my chest hurts and i cant tell if its pneumonia pain or asthma or anxiety or something worse. i dont know if i should wait out the antibiotics, go back again, or go to the er, i just know this doesnt feel like its turning the corner and im exhausted from not sleeping.
target_response:
Kayla, I’m sorry you’re going through this — pneumonia plus steroids, inhalers, poor sleep, and chest pain can feel terrifying, and the fact that you feel worse rather than better deserves prompt attention.

A few important points:

1. **Pneumonia symptoms can linger**, and an X-ray can look abnormal for weeks, but by day 7 of a second antibiotic you should generally be seeing at least some improvement in fever, breathing, energy, and chest discomfort. **Worsening chest pain or “not turning the corner” needs re-evaluation.**

2. **Please verify the Levaquin dose today.** Levofloxacin is commonly prescribed once daily for pneumonia; **500 mg twice daily is not typical** and could increase side effects. Call the prescribing clinic/pharmacy now to confirm exactly how it was intended.

3. Your **burning/gnawing stomach pain** may be gastritis/irritation from antibiotics and steroids. Take steroids with food and ideally in the morning. Levaquin can be taken with food, but avoid taking it close to dairy, calcium, iron, magnesium, or antacids because they reduce absorption. If you have severe abdominal pain, black/tarry stools, vomiting blood, or can’t keep fluids down, seek urgent care.

4. Steroids commonly cause **insomnia, feeling hot/sweaty, wired, anxious, and panicky**, and albuterol can also cause shakiness/palpitations. That said, we should not assume anxiety is the whole cause when chest pain is worsening.

Given your symptoms, I would recommend **same-day medical reassessment**. If your chest pain is worse, you feel short of breath, have trouble speaking/walking, oxygen is <92–94% if you can check it, have persistent fever, fast heart rate, dizziness/fainting, coughing blood, new leg swelling/calf pain, confusion, or severe weakness — **go to the ER now**, not urgent care. The ER can check oxygen, EKG/troponin, labs, repeat imaging, and evaluate for complications like worsening pneumonia, pleural effusion, asthma flare, medication reaction, or blood clot.

If none of those red flags are present, call Dr. Nair’s office today and explain: worsening chest pain despite azithromycin + day 7 Levaquin + steroids, insomnia/sweats, and stomach burning. But if you’re unsure, the safer choice is the ER.

query: my 2 year old has a small fever and is vomiting. i'm worried about her. are these symptoms of the coronavirus? her name is mila rose bennett and her dob is 04/17/2024, shes usually super wild and loud and running around making messes but today shes just kinda laying on me and then randomly throws up, then acts tired again. i know little kids get stomach bugs all the time and im trying not to be dramatic but with everything ppl say about covid i keep second guessing myself and googling which i know is prob the worst thing to do. the fever isnt huge, just small, but its there and the vomiting is what scares me bc she is still so little and cant really explain if her tummy hurts or her throat hurts or anything. she had a few sips of water and then i got nervous bc i dont know if im supposed to keep giving more or wait, and i dont want her to get dehydrated. she doesnt seem like she cant breathe or anything like that, its just the fever and vomiting and her being more clingy than normal. i tried calling but im sitting here with her on a towel and dont wanna miss a call back, my number is 267-555-0198 if that matters. do i need to get her tested for coronavirus or is vomiting not really a sign? should i keep her away from my other kid and my mom just in case? i feel dumb asking but im really worried about her and dont want to wait too long if this is something serious, but i also dont want to drag a puking toddler somewhere if its just a normal kid virus.
target_response:
I’m sorry — it’s really scary when a normally energetic toddler is suddenly quiet, clingy, and vomiting. You’re not being dramatic; you’re paying attention to the right things.

Yes, **COVID can sometimes cause fever and vomiting in young kids**, but vomiting with a low-grade fever is also very commonly from a routine viral “stomach bug,” and sometimes from other infections like an ear infection, strep, or a urinary infection. The fact that Mila is not having breathing trouble is reassuring, but her hydration and alertness are the main things to watch closely.

For now, focus on **small, frequent fluids**. After she vomits, wait about **10–15 minutes**, then offer **1–2 teaspoons** of fluid every few minutes. Pedialyte or another oral rehydration solution is ideal; water is okay in small amounts, but Pedialyte replaces salts better. If she keeps that down for 30–60 minutes, slowly increase the amount. Avoid big gulps, juice, and greasy foods. If she wants to eat later, try bland foods like crackers, toast, applesauce, banana, or rice. If she has fever discomfort and can keep medicine down, acetaminophen is usually okay based on her weight.

I would **keep her away from your other child and your mom for now**, as vomiting illnesses and COVID can both spread. Wash hands well, disinfect surfaces, and avoid sharing cups/utensils. A **home COVID test** is reasonable if you have one, especially if there was an exposure, but a negative test early on doesn’t fully rule it out. If symptoms continue or she develops cough/runny nose, consider repeat testing in 24–48 hours or call her pediatrician.

Please seek urgent care or call her doctor right away if she has: trouble breathing, bluish lips, severe sleepiness or hard to wake, signs of dehydration such as **no urine for 8+ hours**, very dry mouth/no tears, persistent vomiting and can’t keep even small sips down, green or bloody vomit, severe belly pain, stiff neck, rash with fever, or fever lasting more than 3 days.

If she remains tired and keeps vomiting despite small sips, I’d call the pediatrician today for guidance.

query: hi. this covid-19 outbreak is scary. my name is nina patel, dob 02/14/1987, and im prob overthinking but i cant stop reading stuff online and then i get more scared. i got screened this week at riverside urgent care and it was negative. they said dr. omar khan reviewed it or whatever, i didnt even see him long, it was all kinda rushed, and i was trying not to cough in the waiting room bc ppl were staring. but prior to screening i had a week of continuous dry coughs and also throat was sore. not like super bad pain but scratchy and annoying and the cough is dry, no mucus really, just keeps happening when i talk or lay down. i've put myself in a quarantine since before the test honestly, staying in my room, using my own cup, wiping door knobs, avoiding my roommate and trying not to touch the kitchen stuff. i feel silly bc the screen was negative but also what if it was too early or i did it wrong or they missed it? i dont have a fever that i know of, i keep checking and then doubting the thermometer. i called and left my callback as 917-555-3048 but havent heard back yet and im not sure if i should go out again to get screened bc that feels like risking other ppl too. what next? do i still need to screen again? should i just wait more days in quarantine and only go if breathing gets bad, or is the sore throat and dry cough enough to repeat it now?
target_response:
Hi Nina — I’m sorry you’re feeling so scared. It’s very understandable: a persistent cough plus constant online reading can make every symptom feel alarming.

A negative COVID screening/test this week is reassuring, especially since you’ve had about a week of symptoms and no known fever. That said, no test is perfect. COVID can occasionally be missed depending on the test type, timing, and sample quality. Your symptoms — dry cough that worsens with talking/lying down and a scratchy sore throat — can also fit several non-COVID causes, including a regular viral upper respiratory infection, post-nasal drip/allergies, throat irritation, or reflux.

**What I’d do next:**  
- If your test was a **rapid antigen**, consider repeating a home antigen test **48 hours after the last one**, especially if symptoms continue or you had a known exposure.  
- If it was a **PCR/NAAT** and was negative after a week of symptoms, COVID is less likely; repeat testing usually isn’t necessary unless symptoms worsen, you develop fever, or you learn of a significant exposure.  
- You do **not** need to go back in person just for the same mild symptoms if you’re breathing comfortably. A telehealth visit or call-back is a good next step.

Continue staying home while symptomatic and wear a well-fitting mask around your roommate if you need shared spaces. You don’t have to be perfect — your steps like using your own cup, washing hands, and wiping high-touch surfaces are reasonable.

For symptom relief: fluids, honey/tea, lozenges, saline spray, humidifier/steam, and acetaminophen or ibuprofen if needed. If the cough is triggered by lying down, try elevating your head and avoiding late meals; if you have allergies, an antihistamine or nasal steroid may help.

**Seek urgent care now** if you develop trouble breathing, chest pain/pressure, blue lips, confusion, fainting, oxygen levels under ~92% if you have a pulse ox, dehydration, or a persistent high fever.

Also, try to limit COVID searching to one reliable source once daily — anxiety can amplify cough awareness. If you don’t hear back from Riverside, call again or contact your primary care office for guidance.

query: hi, this is maria ellis, dob 03/14/1982, sorry i keep messaging but im just getting anxious bc i dont really understand if im doing enough or if im waiting too long. my chest x-ray reveals i have a small lingular infiltrate.wbc,lymphocytes,hct etc elevated due to infection.i had a course of augmentin duo and steroid.currently on azithromycin 250 mgs.symptoms of pneumonia still persisting.do i need to change my treatment? i saw dr. kevin patel for this and he said to finish the meds but i still feel like the cough and chest tight/heavy feeling and tiredness is not moving fast enough and its making me panic a bit. i picked up the azithromycin 250 mgs from cvs on walnut st and ive been taking it like they told me, not skipping, but i keep checking myself and wondering if the infection is still there bc the xray said infiltrate and my blood work was up. i know antibiotics dont work instantly but im on edge bc i already did augmentin duo and steroid and now another antibiotic and symptoms of pneumonia still persisting. i dont want to over react and run in for nothing but also dont want to sit home if i should be changing meds or need repeat x-ray or different antibiotic. i keep googling and thats prob making it worse. do i need to change my treatment now or should i give the azithromycin more time? like at what point is it considered not working? im just confused bc flu/cold stuff passes but pneumonia feels scary and i dont wanna mess around with it.
target_response:
Hi Maria — I’m sorry you’re feeling so anxious; pneumonia can feel scary, and it’s very common for symptoms to improve more slowly than a typical cold/flu.

A **small lingular infiltrate** on chest X‑ray means there is a small area of inflammation/infection in the left upper lung region. Even when antibiotics are working, the **cough, chest heaviness/tightness, and fatigue can linger for days to weeks**, and the X‑ray often takes **4–6+ weeks** to fully clear. Blood count changes can be from infection, and steroids can also temporarily affect some lab values.

Since you already completed Augmentin/steroid and are now taking **azithromycin as directed**, I would generally **not change treatment immediately** unless you are clearly worsening or having red-flag symptoms. Azithromycin often needs **48–72 hours** before you can judge whether it is helping. “Not working” is more concerning if, after about **3 days on the antibiotic**, you have **no improvement at all**, persistent/recurrent fever, worsening breathing, or worsening chest symptoms.

**What to do now:**
- Continue the azithromycin exactly as prescribed and finish the course unless Dr. Patel tells you otherwise.
- Rest, hydrate, use fever reducers if needed, and avoid smoking/vaping.
- If you have a pulse oximeter, monitor oxygen levels; persistent readings **<92%** are concerning.
- Please schedule/keep close follow-up with Dr. Patel, especially if you’re not clearly improving after 72 hours on azithromycin. He can decide whether you need an exam, repeat labs, medication adjustment, or repeat imaging. A repeat chest X‑ray is usually not done immediately unless symptoms worsen; it’s often repeated several weeks later if needed.

**Go to urgent care/ER now** if you develop: significant or worsening shortness of breath, chest pain/pressure that is severe or not just from coughing, blue lips, confusion, fainting, coughing blood, oxygen <92%, fever ≥103°F, inability to keep fluids down, or rapidly worsening weakness.

You’re not overreacting by asking — but based on what you described, it is reasonable to give the azithromycin a little more time while monitoring closely and following up if you’re not improving.

query: i am a 76 year old female non smoker and non drinker i drink 1 cup of decaf a day, sometimes not even the whole cup because it makes me feel jumpy even tho its decaf. my name is linda marie coughlin and i am just trying to understand if all this lung stuff is connected or if im making too much out of it. in the past year i have had 3 or 4 bouts of pneumonia one was infiltrated, and i just got bronchitis again, and it feels like every time i start to get my strength back i get coughing and tired and scared all over again. i have have been treated with z pack and amoxicillin and i do what they tell me but it just keeps coming back. i am also taking blood thinners due to atrial fibulation and i have a pacemaker so i dont like messing around with infections or breathing things, it makes me nervous especially at night when i hear myself wheeze or cough and then i lay there thinking about it. dr stephen keller follows my heart stuff and silver maple cardiology checks the pacemaker, but i dont always know who to ask when its lungs and infection and the blood thinner all mixed together. i had a pneumonia shot in 2011 .one month after taking the shot i got pneumonia. my brother who is 2 years older had the very same reaction and that has bothered me for years because it just seems too odd to be nothing. our parents were very heavy smoker when we were kids, house, car, kitchen, everywhere, and i never smoked myself and dont drink either so can this be the cause of all my problems now. i just want to know if second hand smoke from all those years can show up like this now or if i should be asking for more testing because i am worn out from being sick so much.
target_response:
Dear Linda,

You are **not making too much of this**. Having **3–4 episodes labeled pneumonia in a year**, plus bronchitis, fatigue, cough, and wheezing, is a good reason to ask for a more complete lung evaluation—especially at age 76 and while taking a blood thinner for atrial fibrillation.

These episodes may be connected, but the cause is not always obvious. Possibilities include:  
- **Incomplete clearing or repeated infections** in the same area of lung  
- **COPD or asthma-like airway disease**, even in people who never smoked  
- **Bronchiectasis**—airways become widened and trap mucus, causing recurrent infections  
- **Aspiration** from reflux or swallowing issues, especially at night  
- **Immune system issues** that make infections more frequent  
- Sometimes heart conditions can contribute to shortness of breath/wheezing, so coordination with cardiology matters too.

Your childhood exposure to heavy secondhand smoke **can increase lifetime risk** of chronic airway irritation, COPD-type changes, and lung problems later in life, even if you never smoked. It may be part of the picture, though recurrent pneumonias still deserve a careful workup.

Regarding the pneumonia shot: the vaccine **does not contain live pneumonia bacteria**, so it cannot directly “give” pneumonia. It is possible you and your brother were exposed around the same time, or already incubating an infection. Also, pneumonia vaccines do not prevent every type of pneumonia. Since your shot was in 2011, ask your doctor whether you should receive an updated pneumococcal vaccine such as **PCV20** or another recommended schedule.

I would suggest contacting your primary doctor and asking for a **pulmonology referral**. Reasonable next tests may include a **chest CT scan**, repeat chest X-ray to ensure clearing, **pulmonary function tests**, sputum culture if you produce mucus, blood counts, immune globulin levels, and evaluation for reflux/aspiration.

Because you are on blood thinners, seek urgent care for **coughing blood, worsening shortness of breath, chest pain, blue lips, confusion, fainting, oxygen levels under 90%, fever with shaking chills, or severe weakness**.

You deserve answers and a plan—not just repeated antibiotics. A lung specialist can help connect the dots.

query:
hi, my name is jason miller and i was diagnosed with pneumonia 5 days ago and went to northside urgent care. been on prescribed antibiotics and i completed the 5 day treatment today, (levofloxacin) and i did take it like they told me, once a day, didnt skip any, even tho it made me feel kind of off and my stomach has been weird. i have flight reservations to michigan on friday morning 4/28/2017, it is Delta Flight DL2876, and im supposed to go because my sister already planned this whole family thing and i already paid for the ticket and hotel and all that, but now im second guessing everything. will it be safe to fly. please advise. your anticipated help will be appreciated.

im still coughing some, not as bad maybe but still there, and i get tired walking around the house, so im worried about being stuck on the plane and also worried about other ppl, like am i contagious still after finishing the meds or is it ok since fever is down. urgent care didnt really explain the flying part, they just said finish the levofloxacin and rest and fluids. i also dont know if the cabin pressure can make pneumonia worse or if sitting that long is a bad idea. i have Blue Cross PPO #BCP-447219 but honestly i dont want another big bill if this is just me being anxious, but i also dont want to do something dumb and end up needing help away from home. i can wear a mask if needed and bring water and cough drops, but if the recommendation is dont fly then i need to know asap so i can try to change it before friday morning. please let me know what signs mean absolutely dont fly, and if being done with the 5 day treatment today is enough or not.
target_response:
Hi Jason — I understand why you’re second-guessing this. Pneumonia can improve on antibiotics but still leave you coughing and fatigued for days to weeks, and flying adds a few stresses: lower cabin oxygen, dry air, and sitting still.

The fact that your fever is down and you completed levofloxacin exactly as prescribed is reassuring. Most people with bacterial pneumonia are much less contagious after 24–48 hours of effective antibiotics and being fever-free, but coughing can still spread respiratory droplets, so a mask and hand hygiene are wise.

That said, **finishing the 5-day antibiotic course alone does not automatically mean you’re safe to fly**—what matters is how you’re breathing and functioning now. Since you’re still getting tired just walking around the house, I’d recommend you **call Northside Urgent Care or your primary care office today for a recheck before Friday**. Ideally they should check your oxygen level at rest and after walking, listen to your lungs, and make sure you’re improving as expected.

**Do not fly / seek urgent care** if you have any of the following:  
- Fever returning or ≥100.4°F  
- Worsening shortness of breath, trouble speaking full sentences, or breathlessness at rest  
- Oxygen saturation below ~94% if you have a pulse oximeter  
- Chest pain, coughing up blood, confusion, fainting, blue lips  
- Severe weakness, dehydration, persistent vomiting  
- New calf pain/swelling or sudden sharp chest pain  
- Severe watery diarrhea, racing heartbeat, or tendon pain after levofloxacin

If you’re **fever-free for at least 24 hours, clearly improving, able to walk around without significant shortness of breath, and oxygen levels are normal**, a short domestic flight is often reasonable. If you do go: wear a mask, hydrate, avoid alcohol/sedatives, bring meds/cough drops, use an aisle seat if possible, and walk/stretch during the flight.

Given your current fatigue with walking, I would not rely on reassurance alone—**get checked before you fly**. If you cannot be evaluated and you still feel winded with minimal activity, postponing would be the safer choice.

query:
hi, with a bad immunity deficiency, how can i keep as safe as possible from contracting covid19 in a family household. im mara bennett and i know ppl keep saying just stay in but its not that simple when its a tiny place and there is always stuff coming in and out. work is not an issue but shopping and accepting deliveries is. also need to take child outsides as home is small, no garden. we are at 42 elm grove, leeds ls6 1ab and its just cramped, like no seperate room really if someone got sick, no garden to let the little one run about, and i feel like im wiping everything then still panicking ive missed handles or packets or the lift buttons.

my mobile is 0786 555 0194 if someone needs it but really im just asking what is sensible and what is over the top. should i be making family leave all shoes/coats at the door, shower after shops, wash every tin and bag, or is that pointless now? deliveries worry me too, like if a driver hands over bags or signs for stuff, do i need to leave parcels outside for hours. i can order from boots on kirkstall rd but even then someone has to touch it and bring it here. i dont want to scare my child but i also dont want to be stupid with my immune system.

if i take child outside, is walking round the block ok if we dont go near anyone, or should we not even touch railings/doors/playground stuff at all. masks inside the home? seperate towels? im trying to be normal but im honestly anxious all day and need plain practical steps pls, especially for shopping and deliveries.
target_response:
Hi Mara — I’m sorry you’re having to manage this in a cramped home; it’s very understandable that you feel anxious. Also, for your privacy, avoid sharing your address/phone in online health messages.

The key point is: **COVID spreads mainly through the air**, especially indoors, not usually from tins, parcels, or door handles. So focus your energy on the measures that matter most.

**Most useful steps**
- Make sure **you and household members are up to date with COVID/flu vaccines** if eligible. Ask your GP/specialist whether your immune condition qualifies you for extra boosters or early antiviral treatment if you test positive.
- For anyone going to shops/indoor crowded places: wear a **well-fitting FFP2/FFP3 mask**, keep visits short, avoid busy times, and wash/sanitise hands after.
- Improve air: open windows when possible, especially after someone has been out or if visitors/workers come in.
- Keep a small “coming in” routine: shoes/coats by the door is fine if it helps, but **showering after shops is usually unnecessary** unless clothes are visibly dirty or you’ve been in a very crowded/close-contact setting.

**Shopping and deliveries**
- Use delivery/click-and-collect where possible, request **contactless drop-off**, and don’t sign shared devices if avoidable.
- You **do not need to wash every tin/bag or leave parcels outside for hours**. Put shopping away, then wash your hands. Wipe only high-touch items if it reassures you, but don’t let it become exhausting.

**Taking your child outside**
- Walking round the block is sensible and healthy. Outdoor transmission is much lower, especially if you keep distance from crowded groups.
- Avoid crowded indoor play areas. Playgrounds are generally okay if not packed; use hand gel after, and avoid face-touching/snacks until hands are cleaned.

**If someone at home gets symptoms/positive**
- Have them wear a good mask, keep windows open, sleep separately if at all possible, use separate towels, don’t share cups/cutlery, and clean bathroom/high-touch surfaces daily.
- Everyone should test if symptomatic.

**Seek urgent help** via 111/999 if you develop severe breathlessness, chest pain, confusion, blue lips, dehydration, or oxygen levels low if you monitor them. If you test positive, contact your GP/specialist promptly about antivirals.

query: hi, my name is jessica ramsey, dob 03/14/1987, and i went to the doctor yesterday and shesaid i had a sinus infection,bronchitis, and i was starting to getpneumonia. it was dr. melissa kwan at northside family clinic, and i was already feeling awful when i went in but i honestly feel even worse now and im getting scared bc i dont know if thats normal or if something is going bad fast. she gave me a shot ofantibiotics and steroids while i was there, like right in the office, and she also wroteme a prescription for bactrim ds andcough syrup. i picked it up at cvs on maple ave and started it like she said, but the cough syrup isn'tworking at all, i'm up all night coughing, like nonstop dry then gross cough, and ihaven't slept for more than 30 min sincesaturday. i keep sitting up on the couch with pillows bc laying down makes me cough harder and then i start panicking bc i cant catch a real break. i feel worse than i didyesterday, more worn out and shaky and my chest just hurts from coughing so much. i dont know if the meds need more time or if i should call the office back or go to urgent care or er. i dont wanna overreact but i also dont wanna ignore pneumonia starting either. i took the bactrim ds and the cough syrup exactly, drinking water, trying steam, honey, all that, nothing is letting me sleep. what should i do? should i wait til morning or call tonight? any advice?thanks!
target_response:
Hi Jessica — I’m sorry you’re feeling so miserable and scared. With bronchitis/sinus infection and possible early pneumonia, it’s common **not** to feel better after just one day of antibiotics; many people need **24–48 hours** (sometimes 72) before improvement. Also, the **steroid shot can make you feel shaky, wired, anxious, and unable to sleep**, which may be adding to how awful you feel.

That said, the fact that you feel worse, can’t sleep, and are coughing nonstop is a good reason to **call the clinic’s after-hours line tonight** if they have one. They may want to adjust the cough medicine, add an inhaler if there’s wheezing/bronchospasm, review whether you need a chest X-ray or recheck, and make sure the antibiotic plan is appropriate for your situation.

Please **go to the ER tonight** rather than waiting if you have any of these:  
- Trouble breathing at rest, gasping, or can’t speak full sentences  
- Lips/face turning blue or gray  
- Oxygen level **<92%** if you have a pulse oximeter  
- Chest pain/pressure not just soreness from coughing  
- Confusion, fainting, severe weakness, or worsening rapidly  
- Fever ≥103°F, or persistent fever with shaking chills  
- Coughing up blood  
- Signs of dehydration (very little urine, dizziness)  
- New rash, facial/lip swelling, or trouble swallowing after Bactrim

If none of those are happening but you truly can’t get any rest and feel you’re getting worse, it’s still reasonable to seek **urgent care tonight** if you can’t reach the office—especially since pneumonia was mentioned.

For now: keep taking the Bactrim exactly as prescribed unless told otherwise; don’t double up on cough syrup or mix it with alcohol/sedatives; stay upright, use humidified air/steam, small sips of fluids, honey, and acetaminophen/ibuprofen if safe for you for chest soreness/fever. If you know what cough syrup it is, a pharmacist can also tell you what OTC meds are safe to combine.

Bottom line: **don’t just tough it out if breathing feels worse**—call tonight or be seen.
